text: DWOW (103.5 FM), on-air as K-Lite 103.5 (pronounced as one-oh-three-five), is a radio station owned and operated by the Advanced Media Broadcasting System. Its studios are located at the AMBS Media Center, 2nd Floor, Starmall EDSA Shaw, EDSA cor. Shaw Blvd., Mandaluyong, and its transmitter is located at Unit 906A, Paragon Plaza Building, EDSA cor. Reliance St., Mandaluyong. The station operates daily from 6:00 AM to 12:00 MN.

Luro is an administrative post in Lautém Municipality, East Timor. It is located in the east of the country and has an area of 125.4 square kilometers. The population was 7,124 in 2015. Luro lies in a valley surrounded by hills and mountains. Rice and corn are grown in the area.
Hugh Cecil Lowther, 5th Earl of Lonsdale (1857–1944) was known as the Yellow Earl and was the first president of the Automobile Association.

The Silver Jubilee Independence Cup was a One Day International cricket tournament held in Dhaka, Bangladesh during January 1998. The tournament was held as a celebration of 25 years of Bangladesh's independence and all the games were held at Bangabandhu National Stadium, Dhaka, Bangladesh.India, Pakistan and the hosts Bangladesh were the participating teams in the tournament.

India were the winners of the tournament after beating Pakistan in the third final of the best of three finals. India successfully chased Pakistan's total of 314/5 in a match that, at the time, was a world record for the highest successful run chase in One Day International cricket. Hrishikesh Kanitkar hit a four (Video)when 3 runs were required off the last two balls to help India chase down the record target and lift the Silver Jubilee Independence Cup.

Saurav Ganguly was awarded player of the match for his century in the third final, while Sachin Tendulkar was named the player of the series.
Gambling Lady is a 1934 American pre-Code romantic drama film directed by Archie Mayo, and starring Barbara Stanwyck, Joel McCrea and Pat O'Brien.

Shigeichi Nagano (長野 重一, Nagano Shigeichi, March 30, 1925 – January 30, 2019) was a Japanese photographer. He won the Ina Nobuo Award in 1986 and had a major retrospective at the Tokyo Metropolitan Museum of Photography in 2000.

Homoisoflavonoids are a class of organic compounds, particularly homoisoflavones and homoisoflavanones, that can be found in various plants, notably in Hyacinthaceae. Some have shown cytotoxic activities towards human cancer cell lines.

W. & J. Sloane was a New York-based retailer of rugs and home furnishings, founded in 1843 by William Sloane. It became the first company to import oriental carpets into the United States. For decades, it was the choice of the elite, decorating prominent homes, creating Hollywood movie sets, and designing automobile interiors. The company filed for bankruptcy in 1985.

Melchior Inchofer or Imhofer, in Hungarian: Inchofer Menyhért (c. 1584 – 28 September 1648) was an Austrian-Hungarian Jesuit. He played an important part in the trial of Galileo, by his arguments, later published in his Tractatus Syllepticus, that Galileo was an advocate of the Copernican system. His role in the Galileo affair is being reassessed in the light of fresh documentary evidence.

Copper proteins are proteins that contain copper ions, which can be classified based on their metal centers. One class, the blue copper proteins, are named for their intense blue color and function as electron transfer agents, shuttling between Cu(I) and Cu(II) oxidation states. The geometry of the copper center impacts its redox properties, and the absence of large reorganizational changes enhances the rate of electron transfer.

Tamika Tremaglio is the current executive director of the National Basketball Players Association (NBPA). Tremaglio was selected by the players to replace Michele Roberts on September 22, 2021, and officially stepped into the role upon Roberts'retirement in early January 2022. Prior to joining the NBPA as executive director, Tremaglio, who retired from Deloitte LLP, served as the Managing Principal for Deloitte's Greater Washington offices.
Leucocyanidin is a flavan-4-ol. It can be synthesized, is metabolized by various enzymes, and is found in many plants.
The 1937–38 season was the 11th season of competitive football in the British Mandate for Palestine under the Eretz Israel Football Association.

Kennicott Grove is an area of prairie and wooded lands that includes the home of John Kennicott (1802–1863) and his family, including his son Robert Kennicott (1835–1866). John Kennicott was an agriculturalist and a doctor. Robert Kennicott was a naturalist and an explorer, who founded the Chicago Academy of Sciences. The grove is 123 acres (0.50 km2) in size. It is located near the intersection of Milwaukee Ave. and Lake Ave. in Glenview, Illinois, in the United States. Kennicott Grove was declared a National Historic Landmark in 1976. The site is maintained by the Glenview Park District as The Grove National Historic Landmark or also The Grove, and contains a nature interpretive center, historic buildings (some original and some recreated), and nature trails.

Nyayangal Jayikkattum (transl. May justice prevail) is a 1990 Indian Tamil-language film written and directed by Sivachandran. The film stars Sivakumar, Lakshmi and Aishwarya. It was released on 1 June 1990.
Alan Brian Carter (born 1952, Lincolnshire, England) is Emeritus Professor of Moral Philosophy at the University of Glasgow.

Newfoundland French ( _français terre-neuvien_ ) is a moribund dialect of French that was spoken on the Port au Port Peninsula of Newfoundland, Canada. It is distinct from the Acadian French spoken in nearby communities such as the Codroy Valley. The dialect arose from a mix of seasonal fishermen from Brittany, Normandy, and the Basque Country, as well as Acadian immigrants from Cape Breton Island and the Magdalen Islands.

While France contested ownership of Newfoundland from 1662 to 1713, most of the French settlement occurred later, despite British disapproval. Contact with English speakers was historically limited, but the establishment of an American air force base in Stephenville in 1940 led to a decline in the use of French. The absence of French-language education and the mandatory attendance of children in English-speaking schools further contributed to the decline. As of 1949, when Newfoundland became a Canadian province, the use of French continued to decline, and today it is spoken by only a handful of elderly residents. There is a movement to reestablish the Newfoundland dialect as the French language of education in the province.

Jasmina Obradović is a Serbian politician and economist. She has served in the Assembly of Serbia since 2012, being elected in the 2012, 2014, 2016 and 2020 elections. She is a member of the Serbian Progressive Party.
Mike Albert is an American guitarist. He has collaborated with various musicians like Frank Zappa, Don Felder, Ruben Guevara, and L. Shankar, and toured with bands like Megadeth and El Chicano.
Russell Springs is a city in Logan County, Kansas, United States. Founded in 1865, it was named for Avra Russell. As of 2020, the population was 26.
Year of the Gun is a 1991 American thriller film directed by John Frankenheimer and starring Andrew McCarthy, Sharon Stone and Valeria Golino.
Leon Mike (born 4 September 1981) is an English football manager and former professional player, who last managed North West Counties Football League side Burscough.
Rémire Island, or Eagle Island, is an island in Seychelles, lying in the Outer Islands of Seychelles, with a distance of 245 km south of Victoria, Seychelles.

William Szathmary (1924–2017), known as Bill Dana, was an American comedian, actor, and screenwriter. He is best known for his character José Jiménez, which originated on *The Steve Allen Show*. Dana also wrote for *The Danny Thomas Show* and co-wrote the film *The Nude Bomb*.

Joseph Buckland (born 27 October 1992) is an English ice dancer. With former partner Olivia Smart, he is a three-time British national junior champion (2012–14) and competed at three World Junior Championships, reaching the top ten in 2014.

Boon Island is a small island in the Gulf of Maine, located off the coast of Maine, United States. It is known for the Boon Island Light, the tallest lighthouse in New England, built in 1855. The island's name is believed to originate from the 1682 wreck of the Increase, where survivors considered their rescue a "boon." Other shipwrecks include the Nottingham Galley in 1710, infamous for cannibalism among the survivors, and the Empire Knight in 1944, which released mercury into the sea. The lighthouse is now owned by Boon Island LLC.

Uncle Jam Wants You is the eleventh studio album by American funk rock band Funkadelic. It was originally released by Warner Bros. Records on September 21, 1979, and was later reissued on CD by Charly Groove Records and Priority Records. It was produced by George Clinton under the alias Dr. Funkenstein. It is the first Funkadelic album since America Eats Its Young in 1972 not to sport a cover illustrated by Funkadelic artist Pedro Bell, though Bell did provide artwork for the album’s back cover and interior. Uncle Jam Wants You was the second Funkadelic album to be certified gold. The album peaked at #18 on the US Billboard 200 and #2 on the US Billboard Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums charts.

Charles Percy Snow, Baron Snow, Kt, CBE, FRSL (15 October 1905 – 1 July 1980) was an English novelist and physical chemist who also served in several important positions in the British Civil Service and briefly in the UK government. He is best known for his series of novels known collectively as Strangers and Brothers, and for The Two Cultures, a 1959 lecture in which he laments the gulf between scientists and "literary intellectuals".

Mark V. Campbell (born 1978) is a Canadian academic, disc jockey and writer. He was raised in Scarborough, a suburb of Toronto, Canada. Currently, he is an assistant professor and Associate Chair of the Department of Arts, Culture and Media, University of Toronto Scarborough. He is the founder of the Northside Hip Hop Archive, an online archive that digitizes oral histories, event flyers, posters and analog recordings that document the beginnings of Canadian hip hop. Campbell is the 2020-21 Jackman Humanities Institute UTSC Fellow and a Connaught Early Career Fellow at the University of Toronto. Campbell was formerly Director of FCAD Forum for Cultural Strategies and adjunct professor at the RTA School of Media, Ryerson University.

Campbell was a Banting postdoctoral fellow in the Department of Fine Arts at the University of Regina. He is a fellow and former postdoctoral fellow with the International Institute for Critical Studies in Improvisation. Campbell became a disc jockey in 1994 and co-hosted the Bigger than Hip-Hop Show on community radio from 1997 to 2015.

Campbell's work focuses on new modalities of being human, sonic innovations within Black music, and the knowledge production of digital archives.

Clarke v Earl of Dunraven [1897] AC 59, also known as The Satanita, was a UK House of Lords case regarding contract law and sailing. It concerned a collision between yachts entered in a race, where one yacht, *The Satanita*, sank *Valkyrie II*. The House of Lords upheld the Court of Appeal's decision that the owner of *The Satanita* was liable to pay compensation.

*Tre Motetter*, Op. 55, FS 139, (Three Motets) is a collection of sacred motets by Carl Nielsen, composed in 1929. They are written in Latin for a cappella choir. Mogens Wöldike, who asked Nielsen to compose something for his Palestrina choir, conducted the first performance on 11 April 1930 in Copenhagen. The texts are taken from the Psalms: "Afflictus sum" (Psalm 38:9), "Dominus regit me" (Psalm 23:1-2), and "Benedictus Dominus" (Psalm 31:21). The motets were published in 1931.
*Circuit Rider* is a 2014 jazz album by Ron Miles, featuring Bill Frisell and Brian Blade. It was released on October 31, 2014, by Yellowbird.
San Michele, locally called in dialect, Gésa Granda, is the Roman Catholic parish church located in the city center at Piazza Dante Alighieri in the town Cameri, province of Novara, Piedmont, Italy.

Stephanie is a character in *The Walking Dead*, portrayed by Margot Bingham. She is first heard as a voice on the radio in season 9, contacting Eugene Porter. In season 11, she is revealed to be a citizen of the Commonwealth. Chelle Ramos was cast as Shira, a spy for the Commonwealth who also uses the alias of Stephanie.
Mike Albert is an American musician and songwriter, primarily known as a guitarist. Active since the 1970s, he has worked in genres including heavy metal, rock, blues, Latin rock, and jazz fusion.

Tre Motetter (Three Motets), FS 139, Op. 55, is a composition for unaccompanied choir by Carl Nielsen. It is a setting of three quotations in Latin from different psalms chosen by the composer and his wife Anne Marie Carl-Nielsen. The three motets, Afflictus sum, Dominus regit me and Benedictus Dominus were first performed on 11 April 1930 at the Ny Carlsberg Glyptotek by Mogens Wöldike and the Palestrina choir, to whom they are dedicated. Among the composer's last works, they were published in 1931 by the Skandinavisk Musikforlag in Copenhagen.

HNA Infrastructure Investment Group Co., Ltd. (SSE: 600515) is a public company involved in real estate and transport, particularly airport and seaport construction and operation. Headquartered in Haikou, Hainan, China, it was originally incorporated on May 12, 1993, as The First Investment and Merchant.

The company underwent several name changes, becoming Hainan Zhuxin Investment in 2008 and Hainan Island Construction in 2011. A subsidiary of HNA Group became the indirect largest shareholder in 2009. HNA Group increased its stake in 2012, holding a combined 39.49% indirectly.

In December 2013, HNA Infrastructure Group became a major shareholder. In late 2015, the company was renamed Hainan HNA Infrastructure Investment Group Co., Ltd. A reverse takeover of HNA Infrastructure Group by HNA Infrastructure Investment Group was completed in July 2016. As of November 2, 2016, HNA Industrial Group owned 60.84% of the shares directly and indirectly. The major shareholders are HNA Infrastructure Holding Group (57.56%) and HNA Industrial Group (3.28%). The ultimate owner is Hainan Province Cihang Foundation.

KGGF (690 MHz) is a commercial AM radio station in Coffeyville, Kansas. It airs a talk radio format and is owned by Sek Media, LLC. The studios and offices are on West 8th Street in Coffeyville.

KGGF has a daytime power of 10,000 watts, covering parts of Kansas, Oklahoma, Missouri and Arkansas. AM 690 is a Canadian and Mexican clear channel frequency. So at night, to reduce interference to other stations, KGGF reduces power to 5,000 watts. A directional antenna is used at all times. The transmitter is a Harris DX-10 installed in 1997. KGGF uses a two-tower array by day and a four-tower array at night, located off Clay Road.

A number of locally-specific units of measurement were used in the Czech lands to measure length, area, capacity and so on. In 1876, the metric system was made compulsory; however, local measurements and old Viennese measurements were still in use.

Milltown (Irish: Baile an Mhuilinn, meaning "town of the mill") is a village in County Kildare, Ireland. The village is in the townland of the same name in the civil parish of Feighcullen It is 7km from the town of Newbridge. It is on the R415 regional road between Allenwood and Crookstown.

The 19th Reserve Division (19. Reserve-Division) was a unit of the Imperial German Army in World War I. The division was formed on mobilization of the German Army in August 1914 as part of X Reserve Corps. The division was disbanded in 1919 during the demobilization of the German Army after World War I. The division was raised in the Prussian Province of Hanover, the Grand Duchy of Oldenburg, and the Duchy of Brunswick.

Vesna Györkös Žnidar (born December 29, 1977) is a Slovenian politician. She served as Minister of Interior in the Cabinet of Miro Cerar from September 18, 2014, to September 13, 2018. She has a law degree from the University of Maribor and a master's degree from the London School of Economics.
Isaac Tomlinson (16 April 1880 – 24 August 1970) was an English outside-right footballer. He played for Chesterfield, Arsenal, Southampton, Portsmouth and Heart of Midlothian.

Ranpak Holdings Corp. manufactures machines and paper products used in protective paper-based packing for shipping goods and merchandise for e-commerce and industry, along with automation solutions. The company is based in Concord, Ohio, and has production facilities and offices in Reno, Nevada; Raleigh, North Carolina; Kansas City, Missouri; the Netherlands; Czech Republic; Shanghai and Singapore. The paper packaging material can be used for multiple applications like wrapping, cushioning, void fill and cold chain purposes, and is sometimes known as dunnage. Ranpak counts customers globally like Amazon, IKEA and Walmart.

The foreign trade of Pakistan comprises exports and imports that contribute significantly to the nation's economy. In the fiscal year 2015-2016, Pakistan's exports totaled US$21 billion, while its imports amounted to US$44.76 billion.

Pakistan's exports experienced substantial growth, more than doubling from $7.5 billion in 1999 to $18 billion in the financial year 2007–2008. The country's export portfolio is diverse, including rice, fruits (kinnows and mangoes), furniture, cotton, cement, textiles, leather goods, sports goods (particularly footballs), surgical instruments, and software. Pakistan also exports manufactured goods such as electrical appliances, Pakistani-assembled vehicles, and defense equipment. Cement is a key export to Asian and Middle Eastern countries, and in 2007, Pakistan began exporting cement to India. Russia is also an important market for Pakistani exporters.

The article also contains a list of Pakistan's main trading partners as of July 2017, and a list of companies that have business ties with Pakistan.

DRUM is a South African online family magazine mainly aimed at black readers containing market news, entertainment and feature articles. It has two sister magazines: Huisgenoot (aimed at White and Coloured Afrikaans-speaking readers) and YOU (aimed at demographically diverse South African English-speaking readers of different ethnicities to inform, inspire and entertain them by offering its own brand of coverage on current events and interesting people).

In 2005 it was described as "the first black lifestyle magazine in Africa", but it is noted chiefly for its early 1950s and 1960s reportage of township life under apartheid. From July 2020 the magazine became an online magazine.

Khan Bahadur Nassakh (1833–1889) was the pen name of Abū Muḥammad ʿAbdul Ghafūr, a Bengali Indian government officer and poet. Writing primarily in Urdu and Persian, his notable works include *Sukhan-e-Shuara*, *Daftar-e-Bemisal*, and *Tazkiratul Muasirin*. The reformer Nawab Abdul Latif was his elder brother. Nassakh served as deputy collector in Dacca and Backergunge from 1860 to 1888.

State Route 159 (SR 159) is a northeast-southwest state highway (signed north–south) in the south-central portion of the U.S. state of Ohio. Its southern terminus is in Chillicothe at the U.S. Route 50 and State Route 104 concurrency; its northern terminus is at U.S. Route 22 approximately 4 miles (6.4 km) southeast Lancaster.

Michael Hugh Meacher (4 November 1939 – 21 October 2015) was a British Labour Party politician who was a Member of Parliament from 1970 to 2015. He served as a junior minister under Harold Wilson and James Callaghan in the 1970s. Under Tony Blair, he was Minister of State for the Environment from 1997 to 2003, one of the longest-serving ministers in the same job in the Labour Government.

Newry railway station (Lang-ga) serves Newry and Bessbrook in Northern Ireland. The station is located in the northwest of Newry, County Armagh on the Dublin-Belfast line close to the Craigmore Viaduct.

The Mercedes-Benz W140 is a series of flagship vehicles manufactured by Mercedes-Benz from April 1991 to September 1998 (sedan) and October 1992 to September 1998 (coupe). It debuted the firm's first passenger V12 engine and other innovations. Available body styles included a 4-door sedan in two wheelbase lengths, a longer Pullman version, and a 2-door coupe (renamed CL-Class in 1996).

Development began in 1981, with the final design by Olivier Boulay approved in 1987. Delays and cost overruns led to the departure of Mercedes-Benz's chief engineer. The W140 was more expensive than its predecessor, leading to slower sales, partly due to competition from Japanese luxury cars. Despite this, the W140 is regarded by enthusiasts as the "last true S-Class" due to its engineering. Updated models were unveiled in 1994 and 1995, with minor cosmetic changes.

The Taguig Integrated Terminal Exchange (TITX) is an intermodal transport terminal under construction in Arca South, Western Bicutan, Taguig, Philippines. The project is owned by Arca South Integrated Terminal, Inc. Ayala Land won the public-private partnership project in November 2015. Groundbreaking took place in January 2018, with an expected operational date of 2020. The project cost is estimated at P5.20 billion. Pre-construction activities were temporarily suspended due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The terminal is projected to serve 160,000 passengers.

Emilie Tolnay (born Emilie Müller; 1893 – 1944) was an Austrian hairdresser and communist activist. She was executed by guillotine for "preparing to commit high treason and favouring the enemy" after supporting anti-government resistance during World War II.

Hostile vehicle mitigation (HVM) refers to security measures designed to prevent vehicle-based attacks. These measures include physical barriers like bollards and Jersey barriers, as well as adapted landscape features. HVM systems are crash-tested to resist penetration by vehicles of varying sizes and speeds, according to standards set by accredited testing facilities. A protective layer of HVM is ideally placed around a building or area, with a standoff distance to mitigate explosive blast effects. HVM is increasingly used to protect crowded events and high-value targets from vehicle ramming attacks, while ideally maintaining pedestrian access.

The Treaty of Worms was signed in 1743 between Great Britain, Austria, and Savoy-Sardinia. Maria Theresa agreed to transfer territory to Savoy-Sardinia, while Great Britain agreed to pay subsidies for Savoy-Sardinia to employ troops. A secret convention stipulated further subsidies from Britain to Austria. The treaty's cession of Finale to Savoy-Sardinia was criticized for violating prior agreements with Genoa. It was presented to the British Parliament in January 1744 and debated the following month.

Alba Vergés i Bosch (born 3 September 1978) is a Catalan economist and politician who is current serving as the First Vice President of the Parliament of Catalonia since 2021 and as Acting President of the Parliament of Catalonia since 2022.
Fernando Pérez Pascal (born October 3, 1964) is a Mexican former professional tennis player.
Sergio Ferrer Marrero (born January 29, 1951) is a former Major League Baseball shortstop.

For example, in Dart, the cascade:

Method cascading is much less common than method chaining – it is found only in a handful of object-oriented languages, while chaining is very common. A form of cascading can be implemented using chaining, but this restricts the interface; see comparison with method chaining, below.
Thomas Grantham (1634–1692) was a General Baptist minister and theologian. He was imprisoned multiple times for his nonconformist beliefs.

*Spirito DiVino* is an album by Zucchero Fornaciari, released on May 27, 1995. It is his first album in three years, after *Miserere*. The album topped the charts in Italy, France and Switzerland, selling over 2.5 million copies worldwide until December 1996.

The king of Rome (Lang-la) was the chief magistrate of the Roman Kingdom. According to legend, the first king of Rome was Romulus, who founded the city in 753 BC upon the Palatine Hill. Seven legendary kings are said to have ruled Rome until 509 BC, when the last king was overthrown. These kings ruled for an average of 35 years.

The kings after Romulus were not known to be dynasts and no reference is made to the hereditary principle until after the fifth king Tarquinius Priscus. Consequently, some have assumed that the Tarquins' attempt to institute a hereditary monarchy over this conjectured earlier elective monarchy resulted in the formation of the Republic.
When World War II was declared in 1939, it had a negative effect on association football; competitions were suspended and players signed up to fight, resulting in the deaths of many players.

Wesley Bolin Memorial Plaza is a public space in Phoenix, Arizona, established in 1978 and named for Governor Wesley Bolin. It houses 30 memorials, including one to the USS Arizona, and has served as a site for protests and demonstrations. Some of the monuments have been the subject of controversy.
John Leslie Sissons (born 30 September 1945) is an English former footballer who played for West Ham United, Sheffield Wednesday, Norwich City and Chelsea.
South Bedfordshire was a non-metropolitan district in Bedfordshire, England, created on April 1, 1974, and abolished on March 31, 2009, when it merged into Central Bedfordshire.

Hydrotalcite is a carbonate mineral with formula Mg6Al2CO3(OH)16·4H2O. It was first described in 1842 in Norway. Hydrotalcite occurs as an alteration mineral in serpentinite. It has been studied for nuclear fuel reprocessing, anion exchange, medical applications as an antacid, and wastewater treatment.

SBI Card (SBICARD), officially SBI Cards & Payment Services Limited (SBICPSL), is a non-banking financial company and credit card provider in India, established in October 1998. It is listed on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and National Stock Exchange (NSE). State Bank of India holds the majority stake. Previously, GE Capital was a major shareholder before exiting in 2017, with The Carlyle Group becoming an investor. In 2019, SBI Card introduced a new brand identity and logo to appeal to millennial consumers. Its headquarters are located in Gurugram, India.

Emilie Tolnay (born Emilie Müller: 6 October 1901 - 5 July 1944) was an Austrian hairdresser who by the time of the German annexation in 1938 had become a resistance activist. She died on the guillotine in the Vienna district court complex.

*Nyayangal Jayikkattum* is a 1990 Indian Tamil-language film directed by Sivachandran, starring Sivakumar, Lakshmi, and Aishwarya. The music was composed by Shankar–Ganesh. It was released on June 1, 1990, to positive reviews.

Newry railway station serves Newry in Northern Ireland. It originally opened in 1855, closed in 1942, and re-opened in 1984. A new station building opened in 2009. It is served by the Enterprise and NI Railway services.

The King of Rome (Latin: rex) was the chief magistrate of the Roman Kingdom. According to tradition, the first king was Romulus, who founded the city in 753 BC. The last king was Lucius Tarquinius Superbus, who was overthrown in 509 BC, leading to the establishment of the Roman Republic. The king possessed absolute power, including military, executive, and judicial authority. He was advised by the Senate, but it had limited power. Kings were elected by the Curiate Assembly, though the Senate played a key role in the nomination process. After the abolition of the monarchy, the title of king was retained for the rex sacrorum, a priest with limited powers.

Ninnes is a locality in the District Council of Barunga West in South Australia, located 16 km west of Lochiel. Established in 1998, it is based on the historic Ninnes Plain, which was settled in the 1860s. The Hundred of Ninnes was proclaimed in 1874, and the District Council of Ninnes existed from 1885 to 1933.

Merewether (/ˈmɛrɪˌwɛðər/) is a former Municipality and today a suburb of Newcastle, New South Wales, Australia, located 3 km (2 mi) from Newcastle's central business district with a population of around 11,000. The suburb stretches 3 km (2 mi) from Merewether Beach in the east to Adamstown in the west.

Waist beads are strings of beads worn around the waist or hips. Originating in Egypt, they are worn today by people from many African cultures, including Ghanaians, Yorubas, and Igbos, each with their own reasons. They can symbolize maturity, femininity, fertility, and well-being. Waist beads are made from materials such as glass, seeds, shells, and gemstones. The purpose and meaning of waist beads are individual to the wearer.

Stephanie is a fictional character from the comic book series The Walking Dead and the television series of the same name, where she is portrayed by Margot Bingham as an alias for Maxxine Mercer while Chelle Ramos was cast as Shira who also uses the alias of Stephanie.
Front Royal is the only incorporated town in Warren County, Virginia, United States. The population was 15,011 at the 2020 census, with an estimated population of 15,155 in 2021.

Ben Christo (born March 22, 1980) is an English musician and songwriter. He is known for his work with The Sisters of Mercy, Night by Night, and Diamond Black. He has also worked as a session musician and with other artists.
Richard Jata (born September 21, 1985 in Dunedin, Florida) is an American soccer player.

*Dejarte de Amar* is the second studio album by Mexican Latin pop band Camila, released on February 9, 2010, through Sony Music Latin. The album was recorded between June and September 2009 at Estudio 19 Mamita Studio in Mexico City, Mexico, and at Henson Studios and Conway Recording Studios in Hollywood, California. It was primarily produced by band member Mario Domm. The album spans Latin pop, pop rock, Latin rock, and alternative rock genres.

The album spawned six singles, including the chart-topping hits "Mientes," "Aléjate de Mí," and "Bésame." "Mientes" was released as the lead single two months before the album and peaked at number four on the Top Latin Songs chart.

*Dejarte de Amar* received positive reviews from critics, who praised its improved arrangements and evolved sound. At the 11th Latin Grammy Awards, the album won "Best Pop Album by a Duo or Group" and was nominated for "Album of the Year." "Mientes" also won two Latin Grammys for "Record of the Year" and "Song of the Year." Commercially, *Dejarte de Amar* reached number one on the Billboard Top Latin Albums and Latin Pop Albums charts in the United States. It was certified Platinum (Latin) in the US and Diamond, 2× Platinum and Gold in Mexico.
*Neopleurotomoides rufoapicata* is a species of sea snail, a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Raphitomidae.
Russell Springs is a city in Logan County, Kansas, United States. As of the 2020 census, the population of the city was 26.
Rose Venkatesan is an Indian talk show host, radio jockey, politician, and activist. She is known for hosting shows such as *Ippadikku Rose* and *Idhu Rose Neram*.
Freerunner is a 2011 action film by Lawrence Silverstein starring Sean Faris, Danny Dyer and Tamer Hassan in lead roles.

AFSCME Council 31 is a labor union in Illinois that is affiliated with the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees (AFSCME). The first AFSCME local union in Illinois was formed in 1942. It has been involved in political action and protests, and has had a standoff with Illinois Governor Bruce Rauner.

Marguerite Louppe (1902–1989) was a French painter, best known for her still lifes and landscapes. Her work synthesized the styles of Cubism and Purism. Louppe was married to the French artist Maurice Brianchon.
Markku Peltoniemi (born 8 February 1948) is a Finnish former footballer and a current sporting director for Helsingin Jalkapalloklubi
**Fernando Pérez Pascal** (born October 3, 1964) is a former professional tennis player from Mexico.

The Bali Botanic Garden (Indonesian: Kebun Raya Bali), officially known as the Eka Karya Botanic Garden, is a large botanical garden located in Bedugul, Bali, Indonesia. Established on July 15, 1959, by President Sukarno, it was the first botanic garden created in Indonesia after the country's independence. Initially intended for the cultivation of conifer plants and as a recreational space, the garden has expanded to 157.5 hectares (389 acres) and now serves scientific, cultural, and conservation purposes.

The garden boasts a diverse collection of over 2300 plant species, including orchids, begonias, bamboo, cacti, carnivorous plants, ferns, roses, and rhododendrons. It also features traditional Balinese medicinal and ceremonial plants. Key attractions include a large orchid display with over 300 species, a bamboo garden with 87 species, and one of the world's largest begonia collections. The garden also provides views of Lake Bratan.

The Bali Botanic Garden is home to various wildlife, including 79 species of birds and treeshrews, with occasional visits from macaques from the neighboring Batukaru Nature Reserve. Three Hindu temples are also accessible from within the garden. The garden attracts over 500,000 visitors annually.

Isaac Tomlinson (16 April 1880 – 24 August 1970) was an English footballer who played at outside-left for various clubs in England and Scotland in the 1900s, spending the largest part of his career with Chesterfield.

A capsule hotel (Japanese: カプセルホテル, romanized: kapuseru hoteru), also known as a pod hotel, is a type of hotel offering small, bed-sized rooms known as capsules or pods. These hotels provide basic overnight accommodation for guests not requiring or not able to afford larger, more conventional rooms.

Capsule hotels vary in size, from 50 or so capsules to 700. Amenities are often communal, including toilets, showers, and wireless internet. In Japan, they may include a communal bath and sauna. Capsule hotels are popular for their convenience and low price, typically catering to men, including Japanese salarymen who have missed the last train home.

Rüdiger Sagel (born 9 August 1955 in Lünen) is a German politician currently with the Left Party and previously with the Alliance '90/The Greens. From 1998 until 2012 he was a member of the state parliament (Landtag) for North Rhine-Westphalia. On 30 June 2012 he and Gunhild Böth were elected in Münster as state spokespersons for the Left in North Rhine-Westphalia.
HD 37974, also known as R126 or RMC 126, is a luminous supergiant star in the Large Magellanic Cloud with a dusty disc.

*The Whole Family* is a collaborative novel published on October 15, 1908 by Harper & Brothers. Conceived by William Dean Howells, it features chapters written by twelve different authors, including Howells himself, Henry James, Mary E. Wilkins Freeman, and others. The novel tells the story of the Talbert family and the impact of daughter Peggy's engagement.

The project aimed to showcase Harper's "family of authors," but the collaboration was not without its challenges. Freeman's introduction of a sexually alluring maiden aunt disrupted Howells's original vision, leading to creative tensions among the contributors. Despite these difficulties, editor Elizabeth Jordan managed to steer the project to completion. While the novel enjoyed a favorable reception at the time of its release, critics have since focused on the interactions and rivalries between the authors.

Bolesław Szymański (born April 22, 1950) is a Polish-American computer scientist. He is the Claire and Roland Schmitt Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute. He is known for his contributions to parallel and distributed computing and networking.
Kate Elizabeth Russell (born 1984) is an American author. Her debut novel, My Dark Vanessa, was published in 2020 and became a national bestseller.

Oxhorn Run is a 1.8-mile-long (2.9 km) stream in Davidson Township, Sullivan County, Pennsylvania. It flows south-southeast and east before joining Painter Run. The stream's source is on the edge of a plateau. Its watershed has an area of 0.87 square miles (2.3 km2). Oxhorn Run is considered to be an impaired stream due to atmospheric deposition and pH.

The Bali Botanic Garden (Lang-id) is the largest botanic garden in Indonesia and is located in the mountainous region of Bedugul, Tabanan Regency, central Bali, around 90 minutes drive north of Denpasar. The Garden was established on 15 July 1959 and is situated around 1300 metres above sea level overlooking Bratan Lake and the Ulun Danu Temple on the slopes of Tapak Hill. The Garden is a centre for botanical research, conservation, education and recreation. It is operated by the Indonesian Institute of Sciences (LIPI).

The Garden has an area of 157.5 hectares (389 acres) and daytime temperatures range from 17 - 25 °C and 10 - 15 °C at night. The humidity averages around 70-90%.

The Garden contains more than 21,000 living specimens belonging to 2,400 species, representing various species from mountainous areas of eastern Indonesia: Bali, Nusa Tenggara, Sulawesi, Maluku and Papua. In addition its herbarium contains 10,000 preserved plant specimens ranging from algae to flowering plants.

Apart from plant collections that include orchids, ferns, cacti and carnivorous plants, there is also a traditional Balinese style guesthouse that functions as a guest house for visitors. One of the world's largest displays of begonias is also on display in the conservatory building.

Callington was a rotten borough in Cornwall which returned two Members of Parliament to the House of Commons in the English and later British Parliament from 1585 to 1832, when it was abolished by the Reform Act 1832.

Parmalat S.p.A. is an Italian global food and dairy corporation. It was founded in 1961 by Calisto Tanzi in Collecchio, near Parma. By the 1990s, it had grown into a multinational corporation, listed on the Milan stock exchange, with over 30,000 employees and a valuation of €3.7 billion.

In December 2003, Parmalat collapsed in a financial scandal involving a €14 billion hole in its accounts, one of the largest bankruptcies in history. Calisto Tanzi was charged with financial fraud and sentenced to 10 years in prison.

Following the scandal, Parmalat was restructured. By 2011, it had amassed €1.5 billion in cash. The French multinational Lactalis launched a successful takeover bid, acquiring 80% of shares in July 2011. Lactalis delisted Parmalat in March 2019 and moved strategic activities to France. In 2015, Parmalat's revenues reached €6.42 billion.

Łęczyca is a town in central Poland, with a population of 13,587 as of December 2021. One of Poland's oldest cities, it was the site of the first recorded meeting of the Polish parliament, the Sejm, in 1180.

Jasmina Obradović (Serbian Cyrillic: Јасмина Обрадовић; born 29 January 1961) is a politician in Serbia. She has served in the National Assembly of Serbia since 2012 as a member of the Serbian Progressive Party.
Lincoln is an unincorporated community and census-designated place (CDP) in Lewis and Clark County, Montana, United States. As of the 2010 census, the population was 1,013.

Whitland Abbey (Welsh: *Ty Gwyn ar Daf*) was a medieval monastery located near Whitland in Carmarthenshire, Wales. Originally a Cambro-Norman foundation in 1140, it came under the patronage of Rhys ap Gruffudd, Prince of Deheubarth. Whitland became an important cultural center and supporter of Welsh independence. It founded several daughter houses, including Strata Florida Abbey and Strata Marcella Abbey. The monastery flourished until the Dissolution of the Monasteries, after which the site was granted to private owners. A forge was later established on the site, and a Victorian house was built. The foundations of the walls are still visible.

*Alice's Restaurant* is a 1969 American comedy-drama film directed by Arthur Penn, based on Arlo Guthrie's 1967 song "Alice's Restaurant Massacree." The film stars Guthrie as himself, alongside Pat Quinn as Alice Brock and James Broderick as Ray Brock. The film follows Arlo as he navigates life in the 1960s, from avoiding the draft to living in a deconsecrated church with friends in Great Barrington, Massachusetts.

The plot incorporates the events of the song, including Arlo's arrest for littering after Thanksgiving dinner, which later affects his draft eligibility. The film explores themes of counterculture, community, and the Vietnam War era. Real-life figures such as Alice Brock and Officer William Obanhein appear in the film, though many of the events and characters are fictionalized. *Alice's Restaurant* was released on August 19, 1969, by United Artists and grossed $6.1 million in North American theatrical rentals.
Maria Clara Eimmart (1676–1707) was a German astronomer and engraver. She is best known for her astronomical illustrations, particularly of the phases of the Moon.
"**Christians, awake, salute the happy morn**" is an English Christmas hymn with text adapted from a poem by John Byrom, and set to the tune "Yorkshire".
Kate Elizabeth Russell is an American author. Her debut novel, *My Dark Vanessa*, was a national bestseller that generated controversy and for which she was shortlisted for the Dylan Thomas Prize.

Dejarte de Amar (English: Stop Loving You) is the second studio album recorded by Mexican pop rock band Camila, It was released by Sony Music Latin on February 9, 2010 (see 2010 in music) Produced by band leader Mario Domm and mostly written by Domm and Mónica Velez, the album became a success in the United States and Mexico. The album was certified Disco Platino in the United States by the RIAA and 4× Platinum in Mexico by AMPROFON. It was recorded in Mexico at Mamita Studio with theme about love, loneliness and lies. The album spawned three singles: "Mientes", "Aléjate de Mi", and "Bésame", all of which reached number-one on the Billboard Latin Pop Airplay chart. The album received generally positive reviews for its arrangements and compositions.

The album reached number-one on the Billboard Top Latin Albums and Latin Pop Albums in the United States and number-one on the Mexican Albums Chart. It received a Latin Grammy award for Best Pop Vocal Album by a Duo or Group and a nomination for Album of the Year. The lead single, "Mientes", received two Latin Grammy awards for Song of the Year and Record of the Year. The album is currently nominated a Lo Nuestro award for "Latin Pop Album of the Year". Mientes was featured in the telenovela Corazón Salvaje (2009-2010) starring Eduardo Yáñez and Aracely Arámbula. Bésame was also featured in the telenovela Triunfo del Amor (2010-2011).

Luro, officially Luro Administrative Post (Lang-pt, Lang-tet), is an administrative post (and was formerly a subdistrict) in Lautém municipality, East Timor; Luro is also the name of two of its towns (Upper and Lower Luro).

The main river of the administrative post is the Adafuro, running between Wairoque and Cotamutu and through Afabubu. Principal crops grown in the subdistrict include corn, rice, coconuts, cassava and other vegetables.

*The Lady in the Lake* is a 1943 detective novel by Raymond Chandler, the fourth in his Philip Marlowe series. Marlowe is hired by a wealthy businessman to find his estranged wife, leading to multiple murders and mistaken identities. The novel draws on Chandler's earlier short stories and is noted for its complex plot and ambiguous characters. It has been adapted into a film (1947) and several radio dramas.

Icon is a compilation album by American country artist Trisha Yearwood. It was released on August 31, 2010 via MCA Nashville Records and charted on the Billboard country albums chart. It was one of several compilations released by MCA following Yearwood's departure from the label in 2007. It contained a series of Yearwood's biggest hits from her years at the label.

Wergea (Lang-nl) is a village in Leeuwarden municipality in the province of Friesland, the Netherlands. It had a population of around 1,560 in January 2017.

There are two windmills in the village, De Hempenserpoldermolen and a paaltjasker.

Korzkiew Castle - a fourteenth-century Romanesque castle located in Korzkiew, Lesser Poland Voivodeship in Poland. The castle is part of the Trail of the Eagles' Nests tourist route. The castle complex includes the castle ruins, housing residence, a grange and park, enlisted on the register of objects of cultural heritage of the Lesser Poland Voivodeship.
*Year of the Gun* is a 1991 American political thriller film directed by John Frankenheimer, starring Andrew McCarthy, Valeria Golino, and Sharon Stone.
Hassan Askiali Haskins Jr. (born November 26, 1999) is an American football running back for the Tennessee Titans of the National Football League (NFL). He played college football at Michigan.

Brontotheriidae is a family of extinct mammals belonging to the order Perissodactyla, the order that includes horses, rhinoceroses, and tapirs. Superficially, they looked rather like rhinos, although they were actually more closely related to horses; Equidae and Brontotheriidae make up the suborder Hippomorpha. They lived around 56–34 million years ago, until the very close of the Eocene.

Ebenezer Norman (born June 10, 1981) is a Liberian activist known for his humanitarian and educational work. Following the Second Liberian Civil War, he founded NDHope, an organization focused on building and managing schools in Liberia. Despite challenges, including the Ebola outbreak and school destruction, Norman rebuilt and expanded his efforts with support from figures like Leymah Gbowee. He is a Global Goodwill Ambassador and LOOP Award recipient.

The Moldavian Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic (Lang-ro, Moldovan Cyrillic: Република Аутономэ Советикэ Cочиалистэ Молдовеняскэ; Lang-uk), shortened to Moldavian ASSR, was an autonomous republic of the Ukrainian SSR between 12 October 1924 and 2 August 1940, encompassing the modern territory of Transnistria (today de jure in Moldova, but de facto functioning as an independent state; see Transnistria conflict) as well as much of the present-day Podilsk Raion of Ukraine. It was an artificial political creation inspired by the Bolshevik nationalities policy in the context of the loss of larger Bessarabia to Romania in April 1918. In such a manner, the Bolshevik leadership tried to radicalize pro-Soviet feelings in Bessarabia with a goal to return it in the presence of favorable conditions and creation of geopolitical "place d'armes" (bridgehead) to execute a breakthrough in the Balkan direction by projecting influence upon Romanian Bessarabia, which was eventually occupied and annexed in 1940 after the signing of the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact.

Jamiul Uloom Jawadia, also known as Jawadia Arabic College, is a Deeni Madarasa (religious educational institution) of Shia Muslims in Banaras (Varanasi) India. Major course of studies include Jurisprudence, Theology and Islamic Literature.

The social season, or season, refers to the traditional annual period in the spring and summer when it is customary for members of the social elite of British society to hold balls, dinner parties and charity events. Until the First World War, it was also the appropriate time to be resident in the city (generally meaning London) rather than in the country in order to attend such events.

In modern times in the United Kingdom, "the Season" is known to encompass various prestigious events that take place during the spring and summer. According to The Sloaney magazine's online guide "Sloaney Season", it starts with Cheltenham Festival (March), and includes the Grand National (April), The Boat Race (April), Badminton Horse Trials (May), Chelsea Flower Show (May), Epsom Derby (June), Royal Ascot (June), Test matches at Lord's (July), Wimbledon (July), Henley Royal Regatta (July), Edinburgh International Festival (August) and others, ending with Goodwood Revival (September).

The 1523 papal conclave elected Cardinal Giulio de' Medici as Pope Clement VII to succeed Pope Adrian VI. According to conclave historian Baumgartner, the conclave was the "last conclave of the Renaissance".

The Whole Family: a Novel by Twelve Authors (1908) is a collaborative novel told in twelve chapters, each by a different author. This unusual project was conceived by novelist William Dean Howells and carried out under the direction of Harper's Bazaar editor Elizabeth Jordan, who (like Howells) would write one of the chapters herself. Howells's idea for the novel was to show how an engagement or marriage would affect and be affected by an entire family. The project became somewhat curious for the way the authors'contentious interrelationships mirrored the sometimes dysfunctional family they described in their chapters. Howells had hoped Mark Twain would be one of the authors, but Twain did not participate. Other than Howells himself, Henry James was probably the best-known author to contribute. The novel was serialized in Harper's Bazaar in 1907–08 and published as a book by Harper's in late 1908.

*Ajantrik* is a 1958 Indian Bengali-language science fiction comedy-drama film directed by Ritwik Ghatak. It stars Kali Banerjee as Bimal, a taxi driver who lives alone in a small provincial town. His taxi, a battered 1920 Chevrolet jalopy named Jagaddal, is his only companion. The film depicts episodes from Bimal's life as he drives people around the industrial wasteland. The film explores the conflict between nature and mechanized civilization through Bimal's love for his taxi.

Quintus Pompeius Falco (c. 70 – after 140 AD) was a Roman senator and general of the early 2nd century AD. He was governor of several provinces, most notably Roman Britain, where he hosted a visit to the province by the Emperor Hadrian in the last year. Falco achieved the rank of suffect consul for the nundinium of September to December 108 with Marcus Titius Lustricus Bruttianus as his colleague.

Arkady Adamovich Brish (May 14, 1917 – March 19, 2016) was a Soviet and Russian physicist, a designer of nuclear weapons, Doctor of Sciences, professor, Hero of Socialist Labour, Laureate of the Lenin Prize and the USSR State Prize.

The Navy Midshipmen men's lacrosse team represents the United States Naval Academy in National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I men's lacrosse. Navy currently competes as a member of the Patriot League and play their home games at Navy–Marine Corps Memorial Stadium in Annapolis, Maryland. During the 20th century, the Midshipmen secured 17 national championships, including 2 United States Intercollegiate Lacrosse Association titles and 15 Wingate Memorial Trophy awards. During the 1960s, a period of dominance for the Midshipmen, they won eight consecutive titles. The program's main rivals include Army, Maryland, and Johns Hopkins.

Eugène-René Poubelle (1831–1907) was a French lawyer, diplomat, and administrator. As préfet of the Seine département, he mandated the use of covered containers for household refuse in 1884. These containers became known as "poubelles," and the term is still used in French to refer to a trash can.
The Design Museum Den Bosch (previously: Stedelijk Museum 's-Hertogenbosch, SM's) is a museum for modern art in 's-Hertogenbosch, The Netherlands.

Newfoundland French or Newfoundland Peninsular French (Lang-fr), refers to the French spoken on the Port au Port Peninsula (part of the so-called “French Shore”) of Newfoundland. The francophones of the region can trace their origins to Continental French fishermen who settled in the late 1800s and early 1900s, rather than the Québécois. Some Acadians of the Maritimes also settled in the area. For this reason, Newfoundland French is most closely related to the Norman and Breton French of nearby St-Pierre-et-Miquelon. Today, heavy contact with Acadian French—and especially widespread bilingualism with Newfoundland English—have taken their toll, and the community is in decline.

The degree to which lexical features of Newfoundland French constitute a distinct dialect is not presently known. It is uncertain how many speakers survive; the dialect could be moribund. There is a provincial advocacy organisation Fédération des Francophones de Terre-Neuve et du Labrador, representing both the Peninsular French and Acadian French communities.

The Mercedes-Benz W140 is a series of flagship vehicles manufactured by Mercedes-Benz from 1991 to 1998 in sedan/saloon and coupe body styles and two wheelbase lengths (SE and SEL). Mercedes-Benz unveiled the W140 S-Class at Geneva Motor Show in March 1991 with sales launch in April 1991 and North American launch on 6 August 1991.

All models were renamed in June 1993 as part of the corporate-wide nomenclature changes for 1994 model year on, becoming S regardless of wheelbase length or body style as well as fuel type. Diesel models carried a TURBODIESEL trunk/boot lid label. In 1996, the S-Class coupé was renamed again as CL-Class into its own model range.

The W140 series S-Class was superseded by the W220 S-Class sedan and C215 CL-Class coupé in 1998 after an eight-year production run.

Production of the W140 reached 432,732, with 406,710 sedans and 26,022 coupés.
Estelle Desanges (born March 8, 1977) is a French former pornographic actress. She began her career in 1999 and has won multiple Hot d'Or awards.
**Rémire Island**, also known as **Eagle Island**, is an island in the Outer Islands of the Seychelles, part of the Amirante Islands. It has a population of 6.
*The Fury of the Aquabats!* is the second studio album by American band The Aquabats, released on October 28, 1997. It is rooted in ska music, with elements of surf rock and punk rock.
Maria Clara Eimmart (27 May 1676 – 29 October 1707), was a German astronomer, engraver and designer. She was the daughter and assistant of Georg Christoph Eimmart the Younger.

Major General Hurdis Ravenshaw (June 1869 – 6 June 1920) was a British Army officer. He participated in the Chitral expedition, the Tirah Campaign, and the Second Boer War.

During the First World War, he commanded the 1st Battalion of the Connaught Rangers on the Western Front, later commanding the 83rd Brigade and then the 27th Division in Salonica. In December 1916, he was captured by a German U-boat and held as a prisoner of war in Austria-Hungary until the armistice. After the war, he commanded troops in South Africa, where he died in mysterious circumstances in 1920.
*Gambling Lady* is a 1934 American pre-Code drama film directed by Archie Mayo, starring Barbara Stanwyck, Joel McCrea, and Pat O'Brien.
**Alan Brian Carter** (born 1952) is a British philosopher and academic known for his work in political, moral, and environmental philosophy.

Danielson Provincial Park is a provincial park in Saskatchewan, Canada, established in 1971. It is located near the city of Outlook and is governed by Saskatchewan Parks.

The park offers a range of attractions and amenities, including three main campgrounds (Shady Lane, Bayside, and Elmview) with electric hookups, potable water, washrooms, showers, and sani-dumps. Visitors can enjoy a sandy beach for swimming, a boat launch, a picnic area, and a fish cleaning station. Hiking enthusiasts can explore two main trails: The Great Trail, which is part of the Trans Canada Trail, and the Prairie View Nature Trail.

José Antonio Páez Herrera (IPA-es; 13 June 1790 – 6 May 1873) was a Venezuelan leader who fought against the Spanish Crown for Simón Bolívar during the Venezuelan War of Independence. He later led Venezuela's independence from Gran Colombia.

He dominated the country's politics for most of the next two decades once the country had achieved independence from Gran Colombia, serving either as president of Venezuela (1830–1835; 1839–1843; 1861–1863) or as the power behind puppet presidents. He is considered a prime example of a 19th-century South American caudillo, and imbued the country with a legacy of authoritarian rule that lasted, with few exceptions, until 1958. He lived in Buenos Aires and New York City during his years in exile and died in the latter in 1873.

Łęczyca (IPA-pl; in full the Royal Town of Łęczyca, Lang-pl; Lang-de; Lang-he) is a town of 13,587 inhabitants in central Poland. Situated in the Łódź Voivodeship, it is the county seat of the Łęczyca County.
Karlo Matković (born March 30, 2001) is a Bosnian-Croatian professional basketball player for Cedevita Olimpija. He was drafted by the New Orleans Pelicans in 2022.

Eduard Deisenhofer (27 June 1909 – MIA 31 January 1945) was a German commander in the Waffen-SS of Nazi Germany. He was an early member in the SS, and served with the Leibstandarte SS Adolf Hitler and at the Dachau concentration camp in 1930s. During World War II, Deisenhofer served with several combat divisions on both the Eastern and Western fronts, earning the Knight's Cross of the Iron Cross. He held a PhD in political economy.

KGGF (690 AM) is a radio station broadcasting a talk radio format. Licensed to Coffeyville, Kansas, it serves Southeast Kansas, along with parts of Oklahoma, Missouri, and Arkansas. The station is owned by Sek Media, LLC, and is a sister station to KGGF-FM. KGGF's studios are located in Coffeyville.

KGGF broadcasts local news, farm reports, and talk shows on weekdays, with nationally syndicated conservative hosts in afternoons and nights. Weekend programming includes shows on gardening, technology, and old-time radio. KGGF first went on the air in 1930. In 1941, KGGF was reassigned to 690 kHz.
Steve White (born 2 January 1959 in Chipping Sodbury) is a retired professional football forward and manager.

Edward North (born in Kirkby Lonsdale, Westmorland) was a British Conservative Party politician. He was the Member of Parliament (MP) for Nuneaton from 1931 to 1935. He served as a Major in the Royal Armoured Corps during World War II and died in 1942.

The Muarakalaban–Muaro–Pekanbaru railway is an inactive railway section in Sumatra, Indonesia. It was a railway project of two parties in two different times, Staatsspoorwegen ter Sumatra's Westkust in the Dutch East Indies era and Rikuyu Sokyuku of the Imperial Japanese Army during the Second World War.

Of the 246 km long railway, the 220 km Muaro–Pekanbaru segment (also referred to as the Sumatra Railway or Pekanbaru Death Railway) actually came into reality by the Japanese to connect Pekanbaru (now in Riau) to Muaro (now in West Sumatra) in an effort to strengthen the military and logistical infrastructure for coal and troop shipments. It would connect the Strait of Malacca, via the Siak River in Pekanbaru, to Padang (with its Padang Station) via an existing railway from Muaro. The railway was completed on Victory over Japan Day, 15 August 1945. It was only ever used to transport prisoners of war out of the area but quickly became overgrown by the jungle.

Currently only Muarakalaban–Muaro portion of the railway that still exist, though the line and stations are inactive; those assets were owned by Regional Division II West Sumatra of Kereta Api Indonesia. There is a plan by Ministry of Transportation to rebuilt the railway as part of Trans-Sumatra railway project, which would connect four separate railway tracks across Sumatra island.

Bugando Medical Centre (BMC) is a tertiary care medical facility owned by the Episcopal Conference of the Catholic Bishops of Tanzania. The hospital is operated in collaboration with the Tanzania Ministry of Health and Social Welfare.

Braeden Matthew Lemasters (born January 27, 1996) is an American actor, musician, and singer. He began his acting career at age 9, appearing in television series such as *Six Feet Under*, *House*, and *Men of a Certain Age*. He also had roles in films including *Easy A* and *A Christmas Story 2*.

Lemasters is also the lead guitarist and vocalist for the alternative rock band Wallows, alongside Dylan Minnette and Cole Preston. Wallows signed with Atlantic Records in 2018, releasing their debut album, *Nothing Happens*, in 2019, featuring the single "Are You Bored Yet?".
Richard Jata (born September 21, 1985) is an American soccer player.

Hydrotalcite or formerly also Völknerite is a layered double hydroxide (LDH) of general formula Mg 6Al 2CO 3(OH) 16·4H 2O, whose name is derived from its resemblance with talc and its high water content. Multiple structures containing loosely bound carbonate ions exist. The easily exchanged carbonates allow for applications of the mineral in wastewater treatment and nuclear fuel reprocessing.

WU polyomavirus (WUpV), also known as Human polyomavirus 4, is a virus species in the genus *Betapolyomavirus*. It has a circular, double-stranded DNA genome. WUpV was discovered in 2007 via analysis of respiratory secretions from a pneumonia patient. Studies estimate WUpV prevalence from under 5% to over 90%, with primary infection likely occurring in childhood. While initially found in a pneumonia patient, its role in causing disease remains unclear. The virus is named after Washington University, where it was discovered.

Roman (IPA-ro) is a city located in the central part of Western Moldavia, a traditional region of Romania. It is located 46 km east of Piatra Neamț, in Neamț County at the confluence of the rivers Siret and Moldova.

Its name was taken from Moldavian Voivode Roman I of Moldavia. From here prince Roman realized the centralization of Moldavia, the city of Roman being the capital of the Lower Country of Moldavia (Lang-ro).

Mark V. Campbell (born 1978) is a Canadian musicologist and scholar of Black Studies. His academic work focuses on Afrosonic innovation, notions of the human, and hip hop archives. He is a graduate of the University of Toronto, where his thesis was titled "Remixing Relationality: 'Other/ed' Sonic Modernities of our Present" (2010).

Campbell is the author of *Afrosonic Life* (2022), which explores the role of sonic innovations in the African diaspora in articulating methodologies for living the afterlife of slavery. In 2010, he founded the Northside Hip Hop Archive, dedicated to preserving the history of Canada's hip hop community from the 1980s and 1990s. Campbell has curated several exhibitions of Canadian hip-hop archival material, including *Still Tho: Aesthetic Survival in Hip Hop's Visual Art*, *...Everything Remains Raw: Photographing Toronto’s Hip Hop Culture from Analogue to Digital*, and *For the Record: ‘An Idea of the North’*.
Thomas Grantham (1634–1692) was an English General Baptist minister, and theologian. He had access to Charles II of England, and made petitions on behalf of Baptist beliefs.

Yehuda Edwin "Ed" Mirvish OC CBE OOnt (July 24, 1914 – July 11, 2007) was a Canadian businessman, philanthropist, and theatre impresario. He was best known for founding the discount store Honest Ed's and for his significant contributions to Toronto's theatre scene, including the restoration of the Royal Alexandra Theatre.

Ebenezer Norman is a Liberian philanthropist, humanitarian, public speaker, and founder of the education non-profit A New Dimension of Hope (NDHope). He is involved in humanitarian efforts in Liberia and throughout West Africa, notably for efforts to build schools in attenuated or war-torn communities after the Second Liberian Civil War in 2003 and the Ebola virus epidemic in Liberia in 2015.
Sergio Ferrer (born January 29, 1951) is a Puerto Rican former professional baseball shortstop.

Steven Williams (born January 7, 1949) is an American actor. He made his debut in the 1975 film *Cooley High*, and is known for his roles as Captain Adam Fuller on the television series *21 Jump Street* (1987-1991), Det. August Brooks on *L.A. Heat* (1996), and Rufus Turner in *Supernatural* (2008-2016).

Williams has also had recurring roles in *The X-Files*, *Legacy*, *Ambitions*, *Locke & Key*, and *The Chi*. His film appearances include *The Blues Brothers* (1980), *Missing in Action 2: The Beginning* (1985), and *Jason Goes to Hell: The Final Friday* (1993).

Donald Joel Aronow (March 3, 1927 – February 3, 1987) was an American designer, builder and racer of the famous Magnum Marine, Cary, Cigarette, Donzi, and Formula speedboats. He built speedboats for the Shah of Iran, Charles Keating, Robert Vesco, Malcolm Forbes, and George H. W. Bush. Retired President Lyndon B. Johnson owned several 16 ft. Donzi speedboats on his Texas ranch with which he would race his Secret Service agents.

Homoisoflavonoids (3-benzylidenechroman-4-ones) are a type of phenolic compounds occurring naturally in plants.

Chemically, they have the general structure of a 16-carbon skeleton, which consists of two phenyl rings (A and B) and heterocyclic ring (C).

Eskişehirspor Kulübü is a Turkish professional football club located in Eskişehir. Eskisehirspor has a total of six trophies, making them the sixth most trophies holders in Turkey. Founded in 1965, Eskişehirspor competed in the top division of Turkish football for 16 years until they were relegated. After being out of the Süper Lig for 12 years the club won the TFF First League playoffs and were promoted in May 2008. Since being relegated from the Süper Lig in 2016 they have competed in lower leagues.

SBI Cards & Payment Services Ltd., previously known as SBI Cards & Payment Services Private Limited, is a pure play credit card company and payment solutions provider in India. SBI Card was launched in October 1998 by State Bank of India, India's largest bank, and GE Capital. In December 2017, State Bank of India and The Carlyle Group acquired GE Capital's stake in the company. SBI Card is headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana/Delhi NCR and have branches in over 100 cities across India. The company is the only publicly listed pure-play credit card issuer in India.

Robert Prosky (born Robert Józef Porzuczek; December 13, 1930 – December 8, 2008) was an American actor. He appeared in numerous films including *Christine*, *The Natural*, and *Mrs. Doubtfire*, and on television as a regular on *Hill Street Blues* and *Veronica's Closet*. Prosky also had an extensive stage career, most notably at the Arena Stage in Washington, D.C., and in the Broadway production of *Glengarry Glen Ross*.

Joachim Herrmann (born September 21, 1956) is a German politician of the CSU. He has been serving as Bavarian State Minister of the Interior since 2007 and Additional Deputy Minister President of Bavaria since 2008. He was also leader of the CSU in the Landtag of Bavaria from 2003 to 2007.
Talent is a city in Jackson County, Oregon, United States. The population was 6,282 at the 2020 census.

The Fairchild 9440 is a single-chip implementation of the Data General (DG) NOVA minicomputer instruction set architecture (ISA) produced by Fairchild Semiconductor beginning in 1977. DG sued Fairchild, claiming that Fairchild was enticing DG's customers to break their licensing agreements, which stipulated that DG software was only allowed to run on DG hardware. Fairchild counter-sued the next year, claiming DG's licensing broke anti-competition laws.

The 9440 was fabricated using Fairchild's proprietary 3-micron bipolar transistor Isoplanar Integrated Injection Logic process, I3L. It ran at speeds up to 12 MHz, about the speed of a Nova 1200. Fairchild also offered a development suite for the system, the FIRE package.

An updated version, the 9445, was announced in 1978. It implemented the NOVA 3 instruction set in microcode, added built-in hardware multiply and divide, and added addressing for up to 128 kWords. In 1986, DG agreed to pay Fairchild $52.5 million to settle eleven lawsuits in progress. In 1987, Schlumberger sold Fairchild to National Semiconductor, who immediately ended production of the line. Strobe Data contracted IC Designs to continue production of what became the ICD9445. This version was fabbed in 1.25 μm CMOS form and began deliveries in 1990.

Bolesław Karol Szymański (born 22 April 1950 in Pasłęk) is the Claire and Roland Schmitt Distinguished Professor at the Department of Computer Science and the Founding Head of the Center for Network Science and Technology, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute. He's known for multiple contributions into computer science, including Szymański's algorithm.

The 2022 Torneo Federal A was a professional football league in Argentina. The season involved 34 teams divided into two zones, competing in a double round-robin format. The top eight teams from each zone advanced to the final stages, where they were seeded based on their performance in the first stage. Racing (C) won the competition, claiming their third divisional title and securing promotion to the Primera Nacional. Four teams, Atlético Paraná, Deportivo Camioneros, Desamparados, and Juventud Unida (G), were relegated to the Torneo Regional Federal Amateur. A total of 559 matches were played, with 1,151 goals scored.

Brontotheriidae is an extinct family of perissodactyl mammals that existed during the Eocene epoch. The earliest brontotheres were small and hornless, but later species evolved to be massive, with horn-like skull appendages. They likely went extinct due to an inability to adapt to drier conditions and tougher vegetation.

Chenopodium fremontii is a species of flowering plant in the family Amaranthaceae known by the common name Frémont's goosefoot. Both the species' specific epithet, and the common name derive from the 19th century western pioneer John C. Frémont.

It is native to much of the western half of North America from Canada through California to Mexico. It grows in many types of habitat from open desert, to shady forest, at 700–3,100 metres (2,300–10,200 ft).
Nuvilly is a municipality in the Broye district in the canton of Fribourg in Switzerland.

Hilbert College is a private Franciscan college in Hamburg, New York. The college is named after Mother Colette Hilbert of the Franciscan Sisters of Saint Joseph, who founded the school in 1957 to train teachers. Hilbert College enrolls approximately 800 students and grants both undergraduate and master's degrees.

*Chenopodium fremontii*, also known as Fremont's goosefoot, is an annual herb growing up to 80 centimeters in height. It has oval to triangular leaves and spikes of tightly-packed tiny flowers. It flowers from June to October. Several Native American tribes utilize this plant for food, using the greens as a vegetable and the seeds as grain.

The Postcard Killings is a 2020 American crime film directed by Danis Tanović, starring Jeffrey Dean Morgan, Famke Janssen and Cush Jumbo, and based on the 2010 novel The Postcard Killers by James Patterson and Liza Marklund. The film was released on March 13, 2020, receiving negative reviews from critics.

Yankee Dutch crossing is a square dance involving 16 dancers arranged as four pairs of couples in the corners of a square. The dance consists of four identical parts, each rotated 90 degrees. Each part has two sections: one where corner groups interact with adjacent corners, and another where diagonally opposite dancers interact. The dance divides the square into two "teams" of eight. One team traverses the diagonals of the square, while the other performs a grand right and left around the circumference. Dancers alternate between the two teams as the dance progresses. The dance is complex, and it is not uncommon for a square to disintegrate before completion.

Theresa Park (born 1967) is a New York-based literary agent. She discovered and represents several internationally best-selling authors, including Nicholas Sparks, Debbie Macomber, Emily Giffin, Janice Y.K. Lee, Taylor Jenkins Reid, Cecelia Ahern, and Deborah Harkness, among others.

She grew up principally in Germany, Lebanon, and Austria, but attended UC Santa Cruz and Harvard Law School in the United States after graduating from the American International School in Vienna, Austria. She is fluent in German and can speak French.

*The Gateway* (also known as *Curtains*) is a 2015 horror film about Danni, an activist who discovers a portal in her apartment's shower. Her experiments with the portal lead to deadly consequences, including the death of her uncle and the transformation of her friend Tim into a monster.

The Lady in the Lake is a 1943 detective novel by Raymond Chandler featuring the Los Angeles private investigator Philip Marlowe. Notable for its removal of Marlowe from his usual Los Angeles environs for much of the book, the novel's complicated plot initially deals with the case of a missing woman in a small mountain town some 80 miles (130 km) from the city. The book was written shortly after the attack on Pearl Harbor and makes several references to America's recent involvement in World War II.

*Wally Bear and the NO! Gang* is a 1992 side-scrolling game developed by American Game Cartridges and published by American Video Entertainment for the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES). The game follows Wally Bear as he skateboards to his uncle's party, avoiding drugs and alcohol. The game and its characters were originally conceived by Walter J. Marsh of Edutainment, Inc., and were later used by the National Clearinghouse for Alcohol and Drug Information for substance abuse awareness.

Saifuddin Azizi (1915–2003) was a Chinese politician of Uyghur ethnicity. He instigated the Soviet-backed Ili Rebellion against the Republic of China government in Xinjiang. He later held various high-ranking positions in the People's Republic of China.

Select Registry is an association of independent lodging properties throughout North America. Founded in 1972 by travel writer Norman Simpson, the organization maintains quality standards through regular inspections of its diverse membership, which includes bed and breakfasts, urban inns, boutique hotels and small resorts.

Poilão is an uninhabited island in the Bijagós Archipelago of Guinea-Bissau. It is part of the Bubaque sector of the Bolama Region. The island is a sacred site for the Bijagó people, traditionally owned by the community of Roxa island. It is the most remote island in the archipelago.

Poilão is a major nesting site for green sea turtles, with approximately 5% of the global population nesting on the island, making it the largest breeding site in Africa and one of the five largest in the world. The island is part of the João Vieira and Poilão Marine National Park, established in 2000 to protect this important habitat.

The 17th Arizona State Legislature was the state legislature that convened in Phoenix, Arizona from January 1, 1945, to December 31, 1946. The legislature met for its regular session from January 18 to March 9, 1945. The body was composed of 19 senators and 58 representatives, with the Democratic Party controlling both chambers. The Legislature also held three special sessions, the first of which was held from September 10 through September 29, 1945.

Seneca Falls is a hamlet and census-designated place (CDP) in Seneca County, New York, United States. The population was 6,809 at the 2020 census. It is located within the town of Seneca Falls.

Originally inhabited by the Cayuga tribe, the village was incorporated in 1831 and dissolved in 2011. Seneca Falls played a prominent role in the Women's Rights Movement, hosting the first Women's Rights Convention in 1848. The National Women's Hall of Fame was established in Seneca Falls in 1969. The village was named after a series of small falls and rapids on the Seneca River.
Oakbank School is a coeducational secondary school located in the hamlet of Ryeish Green (near Spencers Wood) in Berkshire, England.
Nuvilly (Lang-frp IPA-frp) is a municipality in the district of Broye, in the canton of Fribourg, Switzerland.

*Lodestar* is a folk album by Shirley Collins, released on 4 November 2016. After decades away from music due to dysphonia, Collins returned to recording following a 2014 performance. The album received widespread critical acclaim.

Samira Ćosović (Serbian Cyrillic: Самира Ћосовић; born 19 November 1973) is a politician in Serbia. She was a member of the National Assembly of Serbia from 2020 to 2022 and is currently the deputy mayor of Prijepolje. Ćosović is a member of the Social Democratic Party of Serbia (SDPS) and also of its regional affiliate, the Sandžak Democratic Party.

Khan Bahadur Abū Muḥammad ʿAbdul Ghafūr (1833-1889), better known by his pen name Nassakh (Lang-ur), was a British Indian officer, writer, literary critic and collector. He is best known for his magnum opus Sukhan-e-Shuara (Lang-ur Speech of Poets) which was a biography of prominent Urdu and Persian poets. He organised mushaira in places where he worked; inspiring young Urdu poets in Bengal.

NA-36 (Hangu-cum-Orakzai) (این اے-36، ہنگو-کم-اورکزئی) is a constituency for the National Assembly of Pakistan. It covers the whole of the district Hangu. The constituency was formerly known as NA-16 (Hangu) from 1977 to 2018. The name changed to NA-33 (Hangu) after the delimitation in 2018. NA-36 (Hangu-cum-Orakzai) and Orakzai District were also included after the delimitation in 2022.

The Taguig Integrated Terminal Exchange (TITX), (IPA-tl) originally conceptualized as the South Integrated Transport System (ITS-South), is a proposed intermodal transport terminal in Arca South, Taguig. It is primarily a bus terminal for buses going to and from southern Luzon, Visayas, and Mindanao but connected to the FTI station and the under-construction Metro Manila Subway. Originally scheduled for completion in 2020, as of July 2022, construction has yet to begin as pre-construction activities were temporarily suspended due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic.

*Acalymma vittatum*, the striped cucumber beetle, is a beetle pest of cucurbit crops. The beetles cause damage by feeding on foliage and transmitting bacterial wilt. They are attracted to chemicals emitted by cucurbits, and control methods include pesticides, trap crops, and vermicompost fertilizer. The beetles are also attracted to cucurbitacins, bitter compounds found in cucurbits.
Michael Klein (1959–1993) was a Romanian footballer who played as a left back. He died of a heart attack.

Wafa Mustafa (born in Masyaf, Syria) is a Syrian journalist and activist. Her activism is largely motivated by the disappearance of her father, Ali Mustafa, a human rights activist who was last seen on July 2, 2013.

Mustafa was politically active from a young age, participating in pro-Palestine demonstrations as a child and later becoming involved in protests during the Syrian Civil War. In September 2011, she was arrested and detained in Damascus. Mustafa, her mother, and her sister fled Syria in July 2013, eventually settling in Berlin. She graduated from Bard College Berlin in 2020. Mustafa continues to advocate for information about her father and other detained refugees, including protesting at the Al Khatib trial in Koblenz, Germany, and briefing the UN Security Council on forced disappearances in Syria.

Hostile vehicle mitigation (HVM) is a generic term that covers a suite of anti-terrorist protective measures that are often employed around buildings or publicly accessible spaces/venues of particular significance. The design of these various vehicle security barriers and landscape treatments came about as security authorities across the globe sought to mitigate the effects of vehicle borne improvised explosive devices (VBIED).
