The sorts of places that warrant consideration as potential terrorist targets in need of HVM include: government buildings, airports, large railway stations, sports venues, concentrations of entertainment and crowded night time economy, etc.

Fatima Bhutto (Lang-ur; Lang-sd, born 29 May 1982) is a Pakistani writer and columnist. Born in Kabul, she is the daughter of politician Murtaza Bhutto, sister of Zulfikar Ali Bhutto Jr, niece of former Pakistani Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto and granddaughter of former Prime Minister and President of Pakistan, Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto. She was raised in Syria and Karachi, and received her bachelor's degree from Barnard College, followed by a master's degree from the SOAS University of London.

Bhutto is a critic of her aunt Benazir Bhutto and her husband Asif Ali Zardari, whom she accused of being involved in her father's murder. Her non-fiction book, Songs Of Blood And Sword (2010), is about her family. Bhutto has written for The News and The Guardian among other publications.

The Limestone Coast is a South Australian government region in the state's southeast. It is also a tourism region and a wine zone. The area supports farming, viticulture, forestry, and tourism. Much of the Limestone Coast is low-lying, with a Mediterranean climate. The Naracoorte Caves are a World Heritage Site.

Alvarado is one of the 212 municipalities of the Mexican state of Veracruz. The municipality is part of the state's Papaloapan Region and its municipal seat is established at the homonymous city of Alvarado.

The Mourne Mountains (Irish: Beanna Boirche) are a granite mountain range in County Down, Northern Ireland. The highest peak is Slieve Donard at 850 m (2,790 ft). The name "Mourne" is derived from the Gaelic clan Múghdhorna. Features include the Mourne Wall, a 31.4 km dry stone wall crossing fifteen summits, and forests like Tollymore, Donard, and Rostrevor. Reservoirs such as Silent Valley and Ben Crom supply water to the region. The area is popular for recreation, including hiking, rock climbing, and Duke of Edinburgh's Award expeditions. The mountains are under consideration to become Northern Ireland's first national park. They have also appeared in popular culture, inspiring songs, literature, and film locations.
*Freerunner* is a 2011 action film about eight freerunners forced to participate in a deadly race for a million dollars.

Ginataang ampalaya is a Filipino vegetable stew made from bitter melon (ampalaya), coconut milk, and smoked fish (tinapa). Other ingredients may include shrimp paste, pork, shrimp, and other vegetables and spices. The bitterness of the ampalaya can be reduced by soaking and rinsing the slices before cooking. It is typically served hot over white rice.

In United States tax law, an offer in compromise (OIC) is an agreement between a taxpayer and the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) that settles the taxpayer's tax liabilities for less than the full amount owed. To qualify, taxpayers must meet specific conditions, such as doubt as to liability or collectibility, or demonstrate that full payment would cause economic hardship. The IRS assesses the taxpayer's ability to pay, income, expenses, and asset equity. The IRS requires a partial payment with the OIC, but low-income taxpayers may be exempt. An OIC stops tax levies, but tax liens remain until the offer is accepted and paid.

Anything But Conservative (Lang-fr or N'importe quoi d'autre que conservateur), also known as the ABC campaign and Vote ABC, is a political campaign which aims to defeat the Conservatives in Canadian federal elections. It encourages strategic voting.

Canadian federal elections use a first-past-the-post voting system, which allows a party with minority support among the population to win the majority of the seats in the Parliament. Given the make-up of existing political parties in the 21st century in Canada, the centre and centre-left vote tends to split between the federal NDP, Liberals and Greens, which allows for the Conservatives to win a disproportional number of seats in the parliament compared to their popular support.

It is estimated by ABC campaigners that widespread strategic voting in 2015 federal elections decreased the number of Conservative seats by over 72.

Eduard Deisenhofer (27 June 1909 – disappeared 31 January 1945) was a German political economist and SS-Oberführer in the Waffen-SS during World War II. He held various command positions in SS divisions Totenkopf, Wiking, Frundsberg, and Götz von Berlichingen. Deisenhofer was awarded the Knight's Cross of the Iron Cross for his actions commanding a battle group during the breakout of the Demyansk Pocket. He was classified as missing in action in late January 1945.
Oakbank School is a coeducational free school for ages 11 to 16 in Ryeish Green, Berkshire, England, which opened in 2012.

Braeden Matthew Lemasters (born January 27, 1996) is an American actor, musician, and singer. He is best known for his role as Albert Tranelli in the TNT comedy-drama series Men of a Certain Age. He has made guest appearances in several television series such as, Criminal Minds, ER, House, Grey's Anatomy and Amazon Prime Video's The Romanoffs. He is also the lead guitarist and singer for the American alternative rock band Wallows.

In films, Lemasters has appeared in the thriller film The Stepfather (2009), the romantic comedy film Easy A (2010), the family comedy film A Christmas Story 2 (2012) and the horror film Totem (2017).
Jawadia Arabic College is a Shia Muslim religious college in Banaras, Uttar Pradesh, India. It was founded in 1928 by Maulana Syed Mohammad Sajjad Al-Husaini to provide advanced Islamic studies.
Association football during World War II saw leagues suspended, regionalized, or continue in limited form. Many footballers fought, some died, and Jewish players were murdered in the Holocaust.

Steven Williams (born January 7, 1949) is an American actor in films and television. He is known for his roles as Captain Adam Fuller on 21 Jump Street, Lt. Jefferson Burnett on The Equalizer, Det. August Brooks on L.A. Heat, X on The X-Files, Russell "Linc" Lincoln in Linc's, and Rufus Turner in Supernatural.

Throughout his career, Williams has appeared in numerous films, including The Blues Brothers (1980), Twilight Zone: The Movie (1983), Jason Goes to Hell: The Final Friday (1993), 22 Jump Street (2014), It (2017), and Birds of Prey (2020).

He has been nominated for a Screen Actors Guild Award and one NAACP Image Award.

The Gateway, also known as Curtains, is a 2015 horror film that was directed by Jaron Henrie-McCrea. The film had its world premiere on August 31, 2015 at Film4 FrightFest and stars Danni Smith and Tim Lueke as two activists that investigate a series of disappearing shower curtains in Smith's apartment.

The social season in the United Kingdom, particularly the London social season, evolved from the 17th century, peaking in the 19th century. It was a period when the British elite, including nobility and landed gentry, socialized and engaged in politics in London, coinciding with Parliament's sessions from after Christmas until midsummer. Exclusive events were hosted in aristocratic town mansions, with venues like Almack's playing a secondary role.

The season served political functions and provided opportunities for debutantes to be introduced to society, traditionally through presentation to the monarch. This practice ended in 1958, and Queen Charlotte's Ball, a key debutante event, has been revived without royal involvement.

The traditional season declined after World War I, with more events moving to public venues. Today, events like Royal Ascot and Henley Royal Regatta are considered part of the season, often sponsored by corporations. The season traditionally ends with the Glorious Twelfth of August, marking the start of the shooting season. Many events maintain traditional Western dress codes.

The 1956 Queensland state election was held on 19 May 1956 to elect all 75 members of the Legislative Assembly of Queensland. It was the last successful election for the Labor Party until 1989. Premier Vince Gair retained his position, but was later expelled from the Labor Party.
Francisco Fervenza Fernández (1907–1998) was a Spanish trade unionist and military commander during the Spanish Civil War.

Melchior Inchofer (1585 – September 28, 1648) was a Roman Catholic theologian and writer. Born in Kőszeg, he joined the Society of Jesus in 1607 and taught in Messina. He assessed Galileo's *Dialogue* in 1633, concluding Galileo was a Copernican. Inchofer wrote on Hungarian church history and defended the Jesuits.
Ashley Clements is an American actress known for her lead role in the web series *The Lizzie Bennet Diaries*.
Fitchville Township is one of the nineteen townships of Huron County, Ohio, United States. As of the 2010 census the population of the township was 1,056.

Shade is a 2003 American neo-noir crime thriller film directed and written by Damian Nieman and starring Stuart Townsend, Gabriel Byrne, Thandiwe Newton, Jamie Foxx, Melanie Griffith and Sylvester Stallone. The film follows a trio of grifters who attempt to set up a legendary card shark nicknamed "The Dean". The film had a limited release in the United States on May 7, 2004.

The Ruben M. Benjamin House is a Classical Revival house in Bloomington, Illinois. Built in 1856, it is the only home in Bloomington definitively attributed to John L. Routt, who later became the first governor of Colorado. The house was home to Ruben M. Benjamin, a lawyer who argued landmark cases related to government regulation of private enterprise. The house was added to the U.S. National Register of Historic Places in 1978 and is a contributing property to the East Grove Street Historic District.

Rüdiger Sagel is a German politician. He was a member of the Landtag of North Rhine-Westphalia for the Green Party from 1998 to 2007, when he left the party. He joined the Left Party in 2007 and served in the Landtag again from 2010 to 2012.

Callington was a borough constituency in Cornwall, England, that returned two members to the House of Commons from 1585 to 1832. It was a rotten borough from its inception and was disenfranchised by the Great Reform Act of 1832.

Major Edward Tempest Tunstall North JP (31 January 1900 – 1 January 1942) was a British Conservative Party politician. He was the Member of Parliament (MP) for Nuneaton in Warwickshire from 1931 until 1935. He was killed in action in 1942, aged 41.

The Navy Midshipmen men's lacrosse team represents the United States Naval Academy in NCAA Division I college lacrosse. Founded in 1908, the team is a member of the Patriot League. Navy plays its home games at the Navy–Marine Corps Memorial Stadium in Annapolis, Maryland. The current head coach is Joe Amplo.

Navy has won 17 pre-NCAA era national championships. They have appeared in 27 NCAA Tournament, with 8 final four appearances and were runner-up in 1975 and 2004. The team has also won 5 Patriot League tournament championships and 8 regular season championships.
Roberto Juan Poljak (17 September 1932, Buenos Aires; – 30 May 2019, Baltimore County) was an Argentine biophysicist and immunologist.

Hotel Kazakhstan (Kazakh: Qazaqstan Qonaq Üiı) is a 26-story hotel in Almaty, Kazakhstan. Construction began in 1975, and the hotel opened in 1977. At 102 meters (335 ft) tall, it is built in a zone of high seismic activity. The building is an ellipse-shaped volume, with a central entrance accentuated by a curvilinear canopy. A vertical row of bay windows gives the building lightness, ending with a golden crown attic. The constructive basis is a monolithic reinforced concrete core with transverse reinforced concrete diaphragms.

Since its opening, the hotel lobby has featured a 34-meter tapestry, "The Rainbow of Kazakhstan," created by B. Zaurbekova, I. Yarema, E. Nikolaeva, and K. Tynybekov, who were awarded the State Prize of the Kazakh SSR for their work. The facades and interiors are decorated with marble, granite, and shell rock. The hotel was designated a historical and cultural monument of Alma-Ata in 1979.

*Soul Power* is the debut studio album by American musician Curtis Harding, released May 6, 2014, on Burger Records. Harding recorded the album at Living Room Studios in Atlanta, Georgia. The album incorporates elements of soul, gospel, disco, and indie rock.

Joachim Herrmann (born 21 September 1956) is a German politician. He is a member of the CSU party. Herrmann is an MP in the Landtag, the parliament of Bavaria since 1994. Since 16 October 2007 he has been Minister of the Interior of the state of Bavaria.

Roberto Gari (February 13, 1920 – January 22, 2008) was an American artist and actor. He studied at the Art Students League of New York and became known for his portraits. As an actor, he appeared in Broadway productions, films, and television, including *Guiding Light* and *Strangers with Candy*.

Robert Prosky (born Robert Joseph Porzuczek, December 13, 1930 – December 8, 2008) was an American actor. He became a well-known supporting actor in the 1980s with his roles in Thief (1981), Christine (1983), The Natural (1984), and Broadcast News (1987).

Prosky's other notable movies include Gremlins 2: The New Batch (1990), Hoffa (1992), Mrs. Doubtfire (1993), Last Action Hero (1993), Miracle on 34th Street (1994), Dead Man Walking (1995), and Mad City (1997). His most notable television role was of Sgt. Stan Jablonski on the TV police drama Hill Street Blues.
The 1523 papal conclave took place from October 1 to November 19, 1523, in the Apostolic Palace. Giulio di Giuliano de' Medici was elected pope and took the name Clement VII.

Vesna Györkös Žnidar (born 29 December 1977) is a Slovenian politician who was Minister of Interior in the Cabinet of Miro Cerar between 18 September 2014 and 13 September 2018. She is a member of the Modern Centre Party.
USS R-14 (SS-91) was an R-class coastal and harbor defense submarine of the United States Navy.

The Treaty of Worms was a political alliance formed between Great Britain, Austria and the Kingdom of Sardinia, signed on 13 September 1743, during the War of the Austrian Succession.

It was an ambitious piece of foreign policy on the part of the British government which sought to split the Emperor Charles VII, prince-elector of Bavaria, from French influence, whilst simultaneously resolving the differences between the Emperor, Queen Maria Theresa of Hungary and King Charles Emmanuel III of Sardinia.
Robert Sithole (c. 1944 – late 2005) was a South African pennywhistle musician.

Sendle is an Australian logistics and parcel delivery company founded in 2014 by James Bradfield Moody, Sean Geoghegan, and Craig Davis. It is headquartered in Sydney. In 2015, Sendle claimed to be Australia's first carbon neutral delivery service. In 2017, Sendle won a trademark dispute against Australia Post, allowing them to use the slogan “Post without the office”. Sendle has partnerships with eBay and DHL eCommerce.
Markku Peltoniemi (born February 8, 1948) is a Finnish former footballer and current sporting director for HJK Helsinki.

Hilbert College is a private, Franciscan college in Hamburg, New York. Established in 1957, it offers 17 bachelor's degrees and graduate programs. Hilbert's athletic teams, the Hawks, compete in NCAA Division III.

Jefferson Andrés Lerma Solís (born 25 October 1994) is a Colombian professional footballer who plays as a central midfielder for Premier League club AFC Bournemouth and the Colombia national team. He has also been used as a defensive midfielder and as a right-back.

A youth product of Atlético Huila, Lerma played for three years in Colombia before joining Spanish side Levante on loan in 2016. That same year, Levante bought Lerma outright on a four-year contract. Lerma became Premier League side AFC Bournemouth's club record transfer fee in 2018 as he joined The Cherries for a reported €30 million.

Lerma first played with Colombia's Olympic team, representing them at the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio. He made his international debut for Colombia the following year in a friendly against South Korea. He was part of the Colombia squad that participated at the 2018 World Cup, and represented his nation at the 2019 edition of the Copa América.

Eastern Macedonia and Thrace is one of the thirteen administrative regions of Greece. It was established in 1987 and its capital is Komotini. The region is divided into the regional units of Drama, Kavala, Thasos, Xanthi, Rhodope and Evros. The current regional governor is Christos Metios.

Tamika Tremaglio is an American businesswoman and executive director of the National Basketball Players Association (NBPA). She earned degrees from Mount St. Mary's University, the University of Maryland School of Law, and the University of Baltimore. Tremaglio joined Deloitte in 2010, becoming Managing Principal for the Greater Washington Area in 2017. She has received honors from the Washington Business Journal, Washingtonian Magazine, and the Positive Coaching Alliance.

Nieh Hualing Engle (born January 11, 1925) is a Chinese-American writer, editor, and educator. She co-founded the University of Iowa's International Writing Program (IWP) with her husband Paul Engle and directed it from 1977 to 1988.

"Ghost Town" is a song by American rock band Cheap Trick, released in 1988 as the third single from their tenth studio album Lap of Luxury. It was written by Diane Warren and guitarist Rick Nielsen, and produced by Richie Zito. The song reached number 33 on the Billboard Hot 100.

"Ghost Town" dated back to 1981 when Nielsen recorded a demo of the song during sessions for the band's One on One album. The song remained undeveloped until the sessions for Lap of Luxury when Warren added some contributions to the finished song.

A leg is a gross anatomical structure used for support and locomotion in many animals. The number of legs is a characteristic used to classify many taxa.

The components of a leg vary depending on the animal. In mammals, a leg includes bones, muscles, tendons, ligaments, blood vessels, nerves, and skin. In insects, an exoskeleton replaces the functions of bones and skin. In tetrapods, "leg" refers to the entire limb, though in human medicine it may refer only to the segment between the knee and ankle. Artificial legs can be created as robotic legs or prosthetic legs.

An air pump is a device used to move air from one place to another, typically by increasing the pressure of the air. The earliest air pumps were invented in the 17th century, with notable contributions from Otto von Guericke, Robert Hooke, and Francis Hauksbee. Guericke's pump utilized leather washers to minimize leaks, while Hauksbee developed a double-barreled pump for scientific research.

Air pumps come in various forms, each employing different mechanisms. Air compressors use a motor to store potential energy in a tank, releasing air at high pressure. Bellows, one of the oldest types, use a flexible bag to force air out through a nozzle. Piston pumps, like bicycle pumps, use a piston in a cylinder to create pressure. Diaphragm pumps utilize a springy diaphragm to expel and refill air. Reciprocating pumps use a piston within a cylinder to suck in, compress, and expel air. Rotary pumps use gears to capture and pressurize air.

Air pumps have a wide range of applications, including inflating tires, powering pneumatic tools, and providing air for aquariums. They are also essential components of HVAC systems, ensuring fresh food supply, operational data centers, and indoor air quality in homes, healthcare facilities, and office buildings.

The first season of Idol Puerto Rico premiered on August 7, 2011 and continued until November 21, 2011. It was won by Christian Pagán. The season was hosted by Jaime Augusto Mayol. The winner, Christian Pagán, signed with Universal Music Group, the label in partnership with Idol Puerto Rico, and has released two albums.

The COVID-19 pandemic in Tunisia is part of the ongoing pandemic of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2). The disease was confirmed to have reached Tunisia on 2 March 2020.

Patricia "Patt" Murphy Derian (née Murphy; August 12, 1929 – May 20, 2016) was an American civil rights and human rights activist who fought racism in Mississippi and went on to serve as Assistant Secretary of State for Human Rights and Humanitarian Affairs from 1977 to 1981. She was, remembered The Times of London, "a courageous champion of civil rights who took on some of the world's most brutal dictators in her role as a senior American diplomat."

Germania was a German airline founded in April 1978 as Special Air Transport (SAT), commencing operations on September 5, 1978. Renamed Germania in 1986, it operated charter services and, later, scheduled flights. Germania briefly operated as a low-cost airline named "gexx" in the early 2000s. The airline expanded with bases in the UK and plans to modernize its fleet. However, after experiencing heavy losses, Germania ceased operations on February 5, 2019, and declared bankruptcy. At the time of its shutdown, Germania had a fleet of 36 aircraft and served 42 destinations.

*Kanyaka* is an Indian lifestyle magazine published in Malayalam. It is the third-most circulated women's fortnightly magazine in Malayalam. *Kanyaka* contains recipes, beauty and healthcare tips, and relationship advice. It has been the media partner for beauty pageants such as Miss South India. The magazine has also conducted a film award show, cookery contests, and a novel-writing contest. Claramma Varghese is the chief editor and Toshma Biju Varghese is the managing editor.

Lu Rongjing (born August 1933) is a Chinese politician. He served as the Governor of Anhui from February 1988 to April 1989, and Communist Party Secretary of Anhui from April 1988 to February 1996. Lu also served as chairman of the Anhui Provincial Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference from February 1996 to January 2000. Lu joined the Chinese Communist Party in December 1954.

Charles Percy Snow, Baron Snow (1905–1980) was an English physicist and novelist. He held senior civil service positions and served as parliamentary secretary to the Minister of Technology. Snow is known for his novels, particularly the *Strangers and Brothers* sequence, and his 1959 Rede Lecture, *The Two Cultures*, which discussed the divide between the sciences and humanities.

State Route 159 (SR 159) is a 30.91-mile-long (49.74 km) state highway in the U.S. state of Ohio. Existing since 1923, the route runs from a southern terminus at US 23 Bus., US 50, and SR 104 in Chillicothe to a northern terminus at US 22 near Lancaster. The route was extended at both termini in the early 1970s.

Nutrisystem is a weight loss company that was founded in 1972 by Harold Katz. Its headquarters are located in Fort Washington, Pennsylvania, and it serves the United States and Canada. Nutrisystem provides weight loss, weight management, and nutrition products and services, including telephone, Internet, and mobile counseling for weight control. It is a subsidiary of Kainos Capital.

A 2015 systematic review found that Nutrisystem "shows promise" for short-term weight loss compared to a control group, but noted the absence of studies on long-term efficacy. The Mayo Clinic lists the potentially "prohibitive" cost of the diet as a disadvantage. Obesity specialist Fatima Cody Stanford notes that the diet may complicate social eating due to its distinct dietary program. As of 2019, a four-week basic Nutrisystem plan costs approximately $275.
Phlebiella is a genus of crust fungi in the order Polyporales.
Mohammed Ilyas Cherchar (born January 18, 1992) is an Algerian footballer who plays as a defender for RC Arbaâ. He has also represented Algeria internationally.

Yaser Abdel Said (born January 27, 1957) is an Egyptian-American man convicted of the capital murders of his two daughters, Amina and Sarah Said, on January 1, 2008, in Irving, Texas. For nearly 12 years, he evaded capture, becoming a fugitive and was added to the FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives list on December 4, 2014. He was captured on August 26, 2020, in Justin, Texas.

Said's daughters had previously reported his abusive behavior, including physical and sexual abuse. On the day of the murders, Said lured his daughters into his taxi under the pretense of taking them to dinner, then drove them to Irving and shot them both. Sarah managed to call 911 before she died, identifying her father as the shooter.

After the murders, Said went into hiding, aided by his son, Islam Said, and his brother, Yassein Abdulfatah Said. Both were later convicted of conspiracy and harboring a fugitive. Yaser Said was found guilty of capital murder in August 2022 and sentenced to life in prison without parole. Islam was sentenced to 10 years in federal prison, and Yassein received a 12-year sentence. The case has been the subject of a documentary, "The Price of Honor," and was profiled on "The Hunt With John Walsh."

David Peter Ryan (born 5 January 1957) is an English former professional footballer who played as a goalkeeper. He began his career with Manchester United, and later played for Port Vale, Southport, Northwich Victoria, Hyde United and Chorley. He later worked as a coach at Manchester United.

Auguste Marie Fabre (1839–1922) was a French manufacturer, mechanic and proponent of the cooperative movement. He founded workers' clubs and cooperatives in Nîmes, and helped administer the Familistère de Guise. He co-founded the École de Nîmes, a major force in the French cooperative movement.
San Michele is a Roman Catholic church in Cameri, Italy, documented since 1100. The church was rebuilt between 1583 and 1591 and has undergone refurbishments since. The belltower dates to 1827.
Masʿūd Zazoyo or Masʿūd of Zaz (c.1430/31 – 1509/1512) was a Syriac Orthodox author, hermit, monk and prelate.

Parmalat S.p.A. is a dairy and food corporation which is a subsidiary of French multinational company Lactalis. It was founded by Calisto Tanzi in 1961.

Having become the leading global company in the production of long-life milk using ultra-high-temperature processing, the company collapsed in 2003 with a €14bn ($20bn; £13bn) hole in its accounts in what remains Europe's biggest bankruptcy. Since 2011, it has been a subsidiary of French group Lactalis (with fuller control since 2019). Today, Parmalat is a company with a global presence, having operations in Europe, North America, South America, Australia, China, and South Africa.

Still specializing in UHT milk and milk derivatives (varieties of yogurt, cheese, butter, ice cream, etc.), the group also sells fruit juices distributed under the brand names Lactis, Santal, Malù, and Kyr. Its worldwide operations include almost 140 production centres and some 16,000 employees, with 5,000 Italian dairy farms supplying it. Its shares were listed on the Borsa Italiana.

William Forsythe (born June 7, 1955) is an American actor. He is best known for his portrayal of tough-guy, criminal characters, and has starred in films such as American Me (1992), Raising Arizona (1987), Dick Tracy (1990), Gotti (1996), The Rock (1996), and The Devil's Rejects (2005). He has also played recurring characters in television series such as Boardwalk Empire (2010) and Justified (2010).

Forsythe was nominated for the Independent Spirit Award for Best Supporting Male for his performance in the indie film The Waterdance.

The Jamaat al Muslimeen coup attempt was an insurrection in Trinidad and Tobago from July 27 to August 1, 1990, led by the Jamaat al Muslimeen, a radical Islamist group. Led by Yasin Abu Bakr and Bilaal Abdullah, forty-two insurgents stormed the Red House, the seat of Parliament, taking Prime Minister A. N. R. Robinson and much of his cabinet hostage, while seventy-two accomplices attacked the only television station in the country. The army and police responded, sealing off the Red House. After six days of negotiations, the Jamaat al Muslimeen surrendered on August 1. The coup attempt resulted in 24 deaths and millions of dollars in property damage.

Robert Mark Altman (October 10, 1944 – September 24, 2021) was an American photographer. Altman attended Hunter College at the City University of New York and studied psychology and anthropology. Initially he had no intention of becoming a photographer, and said that the camera he wore around his neck was essentially just a prop to "meet girls". However, after graduation, he opened a shop called the Electric Lotus and displayed some of his photographs on a notice board here. The reaction here was so positive that Ansel Adams ended up taking him on as a photography apprentice.

He was soon hired as a photojournalist by Rolling Stone magazine. Here he was able to capture shots of the Rolling Stones while in the recording studio, and also captured shots of artists such as Joni Mitchell and Iggy Pop at music festivals. He found that being a photographer opened doors to places where others were not allowed, and he was able to closely follow the stars and capture images that immortalised the 1960s culture. Following his early success as chief staff photographer for Rolling Stone he expanded into fashion photography and fine art. He became a television producer/director for KEMO-TV, an independent station in the San Francisco Bay Area. For a decade beginning in the mid-1990s, Altman taught web design and photoshop as adjunct professor for several institutions including San Francisco State University and the University of California, Berkeley.

"Anything But Conservative" (ABC) refers to strategic voting campaigns in Canada aimed at preventing the Conservative Party from winning elections. These campaigns encourage voters to support the progressive candidate most likely to win, regardless of party affiliation.

A notable example is the 2008 federal election campaign led by Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Danny Williams, protesting Prime Minister Stephen Harper's changes to equalization payments. This campaign successfully ousted all Conservative candidates in the province. The ABC concept gained broader traction, with various groups promoting strategic voting to minimize Conservative seats.

In the 2015 federal election, a grassroots ABC campaign contributed to the Liberal Party's victory, with many voters strategically supporting the Liberals to defeat the Conservatives. Post-election analysis indicated that strategic voting significantly impacted the election outcome, particularly at the expense of the New Democratic Party (NDP).
Francisco Fervenza Fernández (Santander, Spain, August 15, 1907– Valencia, January 13, 1998) was a Cantabrian anarcho-syndicalist.

Oxhorn Run is a tributary of Painter Run in Sullivan County, Pennsylvania, in the United States. It is approximately 1.8 miles (2.9 km) long and flows through Davidson Township. The watershed of the stream has an area of 0.87 square miles (2.3 km2). The stream is considered to be impaired by atmospheric deposition and pH. Wisconsinan Ice-Contact Stratified Drift, Wisconsinan Till, and bedrock consisting of sandstone and shale occur in its vicinity.
Front Royal is the only incorporated town in Warren County, Virginia, United States. The population was 15,011 at the 2020 census. It is the county seat of Warren County.

*Uncle Jam Wants You* is a studio album by Funkadelic, released on September 21, 1979, by Warner Bros. Produced by George Clinton, the album is a militant sequel to *One Nation Under a Groove*, with themes of converting listeners to "Funkadelia." The album cover depicts Clinton in a Huey Newton-Black Panthers pose, reflecting the album's martial themes.

*Uncle Jam Wants You* featured the band's last major hit, "(Not Just) Knee Deep," which reached number one on the Billboard Soul singles chart. The album significantly influenced the West Coast hip-hop scene, notably the DJ organization Uncle Jamm's Army. Samples of "(Not Just) Knee Deep" have been used in songs by De La Soul and Dr. Dre.

Ginataang ampalaya, is a Filipino vegetable stew made from bitter melon and tinapa (smoked fish) in coconut milk, bagoong alamang (shrimp paste), and spices. The dish can also be made with pork or shrimp and other vegetables. The dish is characteristically savory and slightly bitter due to the ingredients used. It is a type of ginataan.

Donald Aronow (March 1, 1927 – February 3, 1987) was an American boat designer, builder, and racer. He founded several boat companies, including Formula Marine, Donzi Marine, Magnum Marine, and Cigarette Racing Team. Aronow's boats won over 350 offshore races. In 1987, he was murdered in Miami, Florida, and the case was later linked to business disputes and drug smuggling.
Design Museum Den Bosch is a museum in 's-Hertogenbosch, Netherlands, focusing on contemporary visual arts and design, especially ceramic art and jewelry.
Nicholas N. Cox (1837-1912) was a U.S. Representative from Tennessee, serving in Congress for five terms from 1891 to 1901. He was also a Confederate officer during the Civil War.

Danielson Provincial Park is located at the northern end of Lake Diefenbaker in the southern part of the Canadian province of Saskatchewan. The park surrounds the Gardiner Dam, which was built in 1967 and is among the largest embankment dams in Canada and the world. The South Saskatchewan River flows north beyond the park, towards Outlook and Saskatoon. Saskatchewan Highways 44, 45, and 219 provide access to the park. SaskPower's Coteau Creek Hydroelectric Station is located near the park.

The park opened to the public in 1971 and was named for former MLA Gustaf Herman Danielson. It is one if three provincial parks on Lake Diefenbaker, with the other two being Douglas Provincial Park and Saskatchewan Landing Provincial Park.

Danielson Provincial Park's Visitor Centre is located between Lake Diefenbaker and Coteau Bay, and features numerous interactive displays about the Gardiner Dam and the river systems of southern Saskatchewan in addition to a café and beach.
Lawton is a city in Ramsey County, North Dakota, United States. The population was 15 at the 2020 census.

Valsartan, sold under the brand name Diovan among others, is a medication used to treat high blood pressure, heart failure, and diabetic kidney disease. It belongs to a class of medications referred to as angiotensin II receptor blockers (ARBs). It is a reasonable initial treatment for high blood pressure. It is taken by mouth. Versions are available as the combination valsartan/hydrochlorothiazide, valsartan/amlodipine, valsartan/amlodipine/hydrochlorothiazide, or valsartan/sacubitril.

Common side effects include feeling tired, dizziness, high blood potassium, diarrhea, and joint pain. Other serious side effects may include kidney problems, low blood pressure, and angioedema. Use in pregnancy may harm the baby and use when breastfeeding is not recommended. It is an angiotensin II receptor antagonist and works by blocking the effects of angiotensin II.

Valsartan was patented in 1990, and came into medical use in 1996. It is available as a generic medication. In 2020, it was the 123rd most commonly prescribed medication in the United States, with more than 5 million prescriptions.

*DRUM*, originally titled *African Drum*, was a South African magazine founded in 1951. Initially, it offered a paternalistic view of Africans but soon shifted its focus to the vibrant urban Black townships. During its heyday in the 1950s, *DRUM* chronicled the culture, dreams, and struggles of urban Black life amidst apartheid. The magazine fostered a talented group of Black journalists and photographers, including Henry Nxumalo, Jürgen Schadeberg and Peter Magubane. *DRUM* ceased print publication in July 2020.

Railroad brotherhoods were unions formed in the late 19th century as large railroad systems grew. They focused on insurance, work rules, and pay, securing the Adamson Act in 1916. They declined after a failed strike in 1919.

Dave Upthegrove (born May 3, 1971) is an American politician serving on the King County Council from the 5th district since 2014. A member of the Democratic Party, he previously served in the Washington House of Representatives from 2002 to 2013, representing the 33rd district.

Roman is a city in Neamț County, Romania, located in the historic region of Moldavia. The city is first mentioned in historical records between 1387 and 1392. As of 2021, Roman has a population of 48,644. The city became a railway hub in the 19th century and an industrial center during the Communist era.
**E11EVEN Miami** is a nightclub in Miami, Florida that opened on February 5, 2014, and accepts cryptocurrency.
Mohammed Ilyas Cherchar (born January 18, 1992) is an Algerian football player who plays for RC Arbaâ in the Algerian Ligue Professionnelle 1.

Wafa Mustafa (b. 1989 or 1990 (age 34–35)) is a Syrian journalist and activist who campaigns for the release of Syrian detainees. As an activist and former member of Families for Freedom, Mustafa has extensively lobbied the United Nations Security Council to call for the release of the names and locations of all those that Syrian authorities have in captivity. She calls for all detainees in Syria to be freed, whether they are held by the Assad regime or by opposition groups, though she also supports the public demonstrations that began in 2011 against the government of Bashar al-Assad.

Mustafa also campaigns for international recognition of Syrian refugees and against normalized international relations with the Assad regime.

Jackson Township is one of thirty-seven Jackson Townships located in the state of Ohio. Located in Richland County, it borders several other townships. Part of the city of Shelby is located in Jackson Township. The township is governed by a three-member board of trustees.

Lake Temescal is a small reservoir in the Oakland hills, in northeastern Oakland, California. It is the centerpiece of Temescal Regional Recreation Area, also known as Temescal Regional Park (and originally, Lake Temescal Regional Park). It is a part of the East Bay Regional Park District.

Brodie Smith is a former American Ultimate Disc League player, and current professional disc golf player and YouTube personality best known for his frisbee trick shot videos.

Brodie rose to prominence as a premier ultimate player at the University of Florida, where he won two USA Ultimate College Championships, in 2006 and 2010. He also won two National Club Championships, first in 2012 with Austin's Doublewide, and again in 2014 with Denver's Johnny Bravo. He has played professionally with the AUDL's Indianapolis AlleyCats, Chicago Wildfire, Dallas Roughnecks, and Jacksonville Cannons.

Brodie achieved internet fame in 2011 when his Frisbee Trick Shots video became popular on YouTube. As of July 2018, his channel has over 2 million subscribers and 300 million total video views. Clips of Brodie's trick shots have been featured on ESPN's SportsCenter "Top Plays" segment numerous times and other major media outlets, including the Huffington Post. He has a sponsored line of merchandise produced by DiscStore.com

He appeared in the 28th installment of The Amazing Race alongside Ultimate teammate and friend Kurt Gibson, finishing 5th.

As of 2020 he is a professional disc golf player on the DGPT (Disc Golf Pro Tour). He is currently signed to Discraft.

AFSCME Council 31 is the Illinois state chapter of the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees (AFSCME), a union of public service workers in the public, private and non-profit sectors. AFSCME Council 31 has "100,000 active and retired members", including "approximately 40,000 state employees working in more than 50 departments, authorities, boards, and commissions under the authority of the Governor."

USS *Commencement Bay* was a *Commencement Bay*-class escort carrier built for the U.S. Navy during World War II. She was commissioned in November 1944 and decommissioned in November 1946, later being sold for scrap in 1972.
Leucocyanidin is a colorless chemical compound that is a member of the class of natural products known as leucoanthocyanidins.

Valsartan, sold under the brand name Diovan among others, is an angiotensin II receptor antagonist (ARB) medication used to treat high blood pressure, heart failure, and to reduce the risk of death after a heart attack. It is often used as a first-line treatment for hypertension, similar to ACE inhibitors, thiazide diuretics, and calcium channel blockers. Valsartan functions by blocking the action of angiotensin II, leading to blood vessel relaxation and reduced blood pressure.

Common side effects include dizziness, low blood pressure, and diarrhea. Serious side effects include kidney problems and high blood potassium. It carries a black box warning for fetal toxicity during pregnancy.

Valsartan is available as a single-pill formulation and in combination with other medications like amlodipine, hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ), or sacubitril. Several recalls of valsartan products have occurred since 2018 due to contamination with carcinogens, leading to shortages. Despite these issues, valsartan remains a widely prescribed medication for cardiovascular conditions.

Jackson Township is one of the eighteen townships of Richland County, Ohio, United States. It is a part of the Mansfield Metropolitan Statistical Area. The 2000 census found 3,861 people in the township, 2,774 of whom lived in the unincorporated portions of the township.
USS Commencement Bay (CVE-105) (ex-St. Joseph Bay), the lead ship of her class, was an escort carrier and later helicopter carrier of the United States Navy, used mostly as a training ship.

Copper proteins are proteins that contain one or more copper ions as prosthetic groups. Copper proteins are found in all forms of air-breathing life. These proteins are usually associated with electron-transfer with or without the involvement of oxygen (O2). Some organisms even use copper proteins to carry oxygen instead of iron proteins. A prominent copper proteins in humans is in cytochrome c oxidase (cco). The enzyme cco mediates the controlled combustion that produces ATP.

Alvarado is a municipality in the Papaloapan region of Veracruz, Mexico. Established in 1818, it is named after Pedro de Alvarado. The municipal seat is the city of Alvarado. The municipality had a population of 57,035 in 2020.

The Jamaat al Muslimeen coup attempt was an attempt to overthrow the government of Trinidad and Tobago, instigated on Friday, 27 July 1990. Over the course of six days, Jamaat al Muslimeen, a radical extremist Islamist group, held hostages (including Prime Minister A. N. R. Robinson and other government officials) at the Red House and at the headquarters of the state-owned national television broadcaster, Trinidad and Tobago Television (TTT). On 1 August, the insurgents surrendered. They were charged with treason, but were ordered released by the Court of Appeal. Twenty four people were killed and many more were injured in the coup.

The 1997–98 Silver Jubilee Independence Cup was a One Day International (ODI) cricket tournament held in Dhaka, Bangladesh from January 10 to 18, 1998, to celebrate the silver jubilee of Bangladesh's independence. It was a triangular series contested between India, Pakistan, and Bangladesh. The tournament format involved a round-robin stage, where each team played the other twice, followed by a best-of-three final between the top two teams. India won the tournament, defeating Pakistan in the finals to claim their first title. Sachin Tendulkar was named Player of the Series. Saeed Anwar finished as the leading run-scorer, while Saqlain Mushtaq took the most wickets.

The Queen's Fort Military Museum is a military museum in Bloemfontein, South Africa. Originally built in 1848 to replace Fort Dury, it was named in honour of Queen Victoria. Over the years, it has served as a prison, mental institution, and military hospital. Since 1972, it has operated as a museum depicting military conflicts and the struggle against apartheid.

Kennicott Grove, also known as The Grove, is a 50-acre (200,000 m2) preserve in Glenview, Illinois, that has been designated a National Historic Landmark. It was the home of John Kennicott, a physician and nurseryman who moved to the area in 1836 and established a homestead. His son, Robert Kennicott, was a naturalist who collected plants and animals on the property.

The house on the property was built around 1856 and has seen a number of additions and renovations. In 1973, a real estate group attempted to purchase a portion of The Grove's lands for a residential development project, which prompted the formation of the Save The Grove Committee. The property was listed on the National Register of Historic Places on August 13, 1973, and designated a National Historic Landmark on January 7, 1976.
Paul, Baron De Keersmaeker (born 1929) is a Belgian businessman, politician and chairman of PA Europe.
Hassan Haskins (born November 26, 1999) is an American football running back for the Tennessee Titans of the National Football League (NFL). He played college football at Michigan.

Auguste Marie Fabre (5 February 1839 – 26 December 1922) was a French industrialist. He had utopian ideas and was involved in various cooperative experiments. He was the author of the 1896 booklet Les Sky Scratchers in which he extolled modern technological developments.

Eskişehirspor is a Turkish professional football club based in Eskişehir. Founded in 1965 through a merger of local teams, they were quickly promoted to the First League. The club was a strong Anatolian team in the late 1960s and early 1970s, finishing as Süper Lig runners-up three times and winning the Turkish Cup in 1970. Currently, they play in the TFF Third League. Their home ground is the New Eskişehir Stadium, which has a capacity of 32,500.
Estelle Desanges (born 8 March 1977) is a French former pornographic actress. During the 2000s, she was one of France's most popular porn actresses.

Pridwen is King Arthur's shield in the medieval Arthurian tradition. First appearing in Geoffrey of Monmouth's *Historia Regum Britanniae* (c. 1130s), Pridwen is described as bearing an image of the Virgin Mary. This motif was likely borrowed from earlier texts. Later works, such as Wace's *Roman de Brut* and Layamon's *Brut*, elaborate on the shield's appearance and the placement of the Marian image. The shield is also mentioned in other medieval chronicles and poems, sometimes with additional details.

The 19th Reserve Division was a German infantry division that fought in World War I on the Western and Eastern Fronts. It was active from 1914 to 1919 and had approximately 15,000 soldiers. The division fought in many major battles, including the First Battle of the Marne, the Battle of Verdun, the Battle of the Somme, Passchendaele, and the Oise-Aisne Offensive. Allied intelligence considered the division to be first class.
Lawton is a city in Ramsey County, North Dakota, United States. The population was 15 at the 2020 census. Lawton was founded in 1902.
The 1937–38 season in Mandatory Palestine football saw Maccabi Tel Aviv win the 1937 Palestine League and Hapoel Tel Aviv win the 1938 Palestine Cup.

Benjamin Brackett Downing (born September 11, 1981) is an American politician and businessman. A member of the Democratic Party, he served as a Massachusetts State Senator for the Berkshire, Hampshire, Franklin and Hampden district from 2007 to 2017. In 2022, he unsuccessfully ran for Governor of Massachusetts.

The Wesley Bolin Memorial Plaza is an urban park and gathering place in front of the Arizona state capitol complex in downtown Phoenix, Arizona. One of the Phoenix Points of Pride, it is the site of various memorials honoring prominent figures, wars, and events in Arizona history.

Quintus Pompeius Falco (c. 70 – after 143 AD) was a Roman senator and general who served as governor of Britannia. He held a suffect consulship, commanded Legio V Macedonica, and governed Lycia et Pamphylia, Judea, and Moesia Inferior. Falco also served as curator of the Via Traiana and proconsul of Asia.

Germania Fluggesellschaft mbH, trading as Germania (IPA-de), was a privately owned German airline with its headquarters in Berlin. It began by focusing on charter operations, then moved towards becoming a scheduled carrier, although some charter flights were still flown under the brand. The change in strategy led to growth over its last few years, and Germania operated to destinations in Europe, North Africa and the Middle East from several German bases. It carried 2.5 million passengers in 2009 and had around 850 employees as of summer 2014. It declared bankruptcy on 4 February 2019 and ceased operations on 5 February 2019.
John Graham is a Canadian former Native American activist. He is best known for being the person who shot and killed fellow American Indian Movement activist Anna Mae Aquash.

Poilão is a small uninhabited island in the Bijagós Archipelago of Guinea-Bissau. It contains the country's southernmost point, at 10°51'53"N,15°43'36"W. It is part of the João Vieira and Poilão Marine National Park. The island is heavily forested. The nearest islands are Meio, 7 nmi (13 km) to the north, Cavalos, 9 nmi (17 km) to the north and João Vieira, 11 nmi (20 km) to the north-northeast. Its area is 43 ha. Between ca. 7000 and 29,000 green sea turtle (Chelonia mydas) nests are laid per year at the globally important site of Poilão Island. There is a lighthouse on the island, with focal height 27 m (89 ft).

Ben Christo (born 22 March 1980) is an English rock musician, guitarist, songwriter, vocalist, lyricist and session musician, he is best known for his tenures with The Sisters of Mercy, Night By Night and Diamond Black.
Linda Gottlieb is a film and television producer and educator.

Stephen Langton (c. 1150 – 9 July 1228) was an English Cardinal of the Roman Catholic Church and Archbishop of Canterbury. His appointment as archbishop led to a bitter dispute between King John of England and Pope Innocent III. Langton is widely credited with dividing the Bible into the standard modern arrangement of chapters.
**Rikuchū-Noda Station** (陸中野田駅, *Rikuchū-Noda-eki*) is a railway station on the Sanriku Railway Rias Line in Noda, Iwate, Japan. It opened on 20 July 1975.

Akumaa Mama Zimbi, also known as Akumaa, is a Ghanaian actress, radio and television personality, relationship expert, and philanthropist. She gained recognition in the early 1990s as an actress on the Cantata Show. In the early 2000s, she transitioned to radio, eventually hosting the Odo Ahomaso Show on Adom TV, where she offers relationship and marital advice.

Beyond her media career, Akumaa founded Mama Zimbi's Foundation in 2004. The foundation supports underprivileged women and children, particularly widows, and promotes sexual and reproductive health education. She is a recipient of two National Awards and the Radio and Television Personalities Awards.

Luigino Rinaldi (born c. 1947) is a former politician in Ontario, Canada. He was a Liberal member of the Legislative Assembly of Ontario from 2003 to 2011 and again from 2014 to 2018 who represented the ridings of Northumberland and Northumberland—Quinte West.

WU polyomavirus (also known as WU virus, WUPyV, or Human polyomavirus 4) is a virus of the family Polyomaviridae. It was discovered in 2007 in samples of human respiratory secretions, originally from a child patient in Australia who presented with clinical signs of pneumonia and in whom other common respiratory viruses were not detected. Follow-up studies identified the presence of WU virus in respiratory secretion samples from patients in Australia and the United States, suggesting that, like other human polyomaviruses, WU virus is widely distributed.

Waist beads are a piece of jewelry worn around the waist or hip area. Originating in Africa, waist beads are traditionally worn by women as a symbol of waist size, beauty, sexuality, femininity, fertility, well-being, and maturity.

Commonly made of glass, metal, crystals, gemstones, charms, wooden beads, or plastic beads, waist beads are typically strung on a cotton thread, twine, thread, wire, clear cord, or elastic cord. The colors and materials used may have symbolic, cultural, or personal significance to the wearer.

*The Romance of Tiger and Rose* (传闻中的陈芊芊) is a 2020 Chinese romantic comedy historical drama created by Nan Zhen and directed by Cha Chuanyi, starring Zhao Lusi and Ding Yuxi. The series follows screenwriter Chen Xiaoqian (Zhao Lusi) who becomes trapped in her own script as the despised third princess, Chen Qianqian. To survive, she must manipulate the story and bring the lead characters together. Filmed in Hengdian World Studios, it premiered on Tencent Video in May 2020. The show has been praised for its plot, but criticized for inconsistent gender dynamics.

The Amos Baldwin House is a historic Colonial house at 92 Goshen Street East in Norfolk, Connecticut. Built around 1765, it is one of Norfolk's oldest buildings. The house was added to the National Register of Historic Places on July 19, 2016.

Youngblood is the third studio album by Australian pop rock band 5 Seconds of Summer. The album was initially set to be released on 22 June 2018, but was later brought forward to 15 June. Four singles were released in support of the album: "Want You Back", "Youngblood", "Valentine" and "Lie to Me". The album was released to positive reviews, with critics praising the lyrical maturity and the sound change from their previous work. In support of the album, the band embarked on the Meet You There Tour.

As well as appearing on a multitude of weekly and 2018 and 2019 year-end charts over several countries and receiving multiple certifications, the album's title track, "Youngblood", impacted the ARIA decade-end chart (2010-2019), which ranks the most popular songs of the decade, at number thirty-seven. In the ARIA Australian decade-end chart (2010-2019), ranking the most popular songs of the decade by Australian artists, "Youngblood" ranked at number four. "Youngblood" sold over five million adjusted copies and is the most streamed Australian song of all time on Apple Music. According to ARIA certifications,"Youngblood" is the eleventh best-selling single in Australian history. Youngblood was the most-streamed Australian album of 2018. In February 2020, the band were honored in the APRA AMCOS' The 1,000,000,000 List, for "Youngblood" having been streamed over 1 billion times.

*Idol Puerto Rico* season 1 aired on WAPA-TV from August 7 to November 21, 2011. The show was hosted by Jaime Augusto Mayol, and the judges were Ricardo Montaner, Jerry Rivera, Erika Ender, and Topy Mamery. Christian Pagán won the season, and Japhet Albert was the runner-up.
Circuit Rider is an album by cornetist/composer Ron Miles with guitarist Bill Frisell and drummer Brian Blade which was released on the Yellowbird label in 2014.

The railroad brotherhoods are labor unions of railroad workers in the United States. They first appeared in 1863 and they are still active. Until recent years they were largely independent of each other and of the American Federation of Labor.

William Szathmary (October 5, 1924 – June 15, 2017), known as Bill Dana, was an American comedian, actor, and screenwriter. He often appeared on television shows such as The Ed Sullivan Show, frequently in the guise of a heavily accented Bolivian character named José Jiménez. Dana often portrayed the Jiménez character as an astronaut.

Margaret JoBeth Williams (born December 6, 1948) is an American actress and television director. Born in Houston, Texas, she began her career with a role on the children's television series *Jabberwocky*. She gained recognition for her roles in motion pictures such as *Stir Crazy* (1980), *Poltergeist* (1982), and *The Big Chill* (1983).

Williams has also been lauded for her performances in television movies, including *The Day After* (1983) and *My Name is Bill W.* (1989), earning Emmy nominations for her roles in *Adam* (1983) and *Baby M* (1988). In 1995, she received an Academy Award nomination for directing the short film *On Hope*. She has also had recurring roles in television series such as *Private Practice*.

Williams is married to director John Pasquin, and they have two children.

Akumaa Mama Zimbi, also known professionally as Dr. Joyce Akumaa Dongotey-Padi, is a Ghanaian television and radio broadcast journalist, women's rights activist, blogger, marriage counselor, and an actress in Ghana. Akumaa is known for her fascinating, yet sexually stimulating, ways of expressing "Medaase" (Thank you). Her exceptional ways of wearing her headgear also comes off as a distinct Akumaa brand. Little is known about Akumaa's early life as well as her family and educational background. However, it is known that her father is a policeman and her husband is a businessman to whom she has been married for over twenty-four years

Matthew Daniels (born September 27, 1989) is a former American football safety and current special teams coach of the Minnesota Vikings of the National Football League (NFL). He was signed by the St. Louis Rams as an undrafted free agent in 2012. He played college football for Duke.

Czech units of measurement varied across Bohemia, Moravia, and Silesia. Units of length included the *látro* (1.917 m) and *stopa* (≈0.29 m). The *měřice* and *korec* were units of area, while the Moravian *měřice* (70.6 L) and *korec* (93.592 L) measured capacity.

Elections were held in the Australian state of Queensland on 19 May 1956 to elect the 75 members of the state's Legislative Assembly. The Labor government was seeking its ninth continuous term in office since the 1932 election; it would be Vince Gair's second election as Premier.

Roberto Gari (February 13, 1920 – January 22, 2008) was an American artist and actor. He was born in Brooklyn and appeared in vaudeville under the name of Jackie Hayes from the age of 4. He was best known for his role as Guy Blank, Jerri Blank's father, in the cult Comedy Central series Strangers with Candy.
Talent is a city in Jackson County, Oregon, United States, with a population of 6,282 as of 2020.

The Limestone Coast is a name used since the early twenty-first century for a South Australian government region located in the south east of South Australia which immediately adjoins the continental coastline and the Victorian border. The name is also used for a tourist region and a wine zone both located in the same part of South Australia.

Hainan HNA Infrastructure Investment Group Co., Ltd. formerly Hainan Island Construction Co., Ltd. is a Chinese listed company based in Haikou. in mid-2016 the company acquired HNA Infrastructure Group (Chinese: 海航基础产业集团) in a reverse IPO from intermediate parent company HNA Infrastructure Holding Group (Chinese: 海航基础控股集团), which HNA Infrastructure Group is the parent company of HNA Real Estate and HNA Airport Group (Chinese: 海航机场集团, in turn HNA Airport Group is the parent company of HNA Airport Holdings (Group) (Chinese: 海航机场控股(集团)) and HNA Airport Holdings is the parent company of Sanya Phoenix International Airport Co., Ltd.); HNA Airport Group is the largest shareholder of Haikou Meilan International Airport (19.58% as at October 2016), which in turn the largest shareholder of Hong Kong listed company HNA Infrastructure. In December 2016 a proposed capital increase of the HK-listed company was announced. HNA Infrastructure Investment Group would purchase a minority share directly. Haikou Meilan International Airport was the second-largest shareholder of Hainan Airlines; Hainan Airlines, however, also owned a minority stake in Haikou Meilan International Airport.

The registered office was located in Meilan District, but the general office was located in West Zone 21/F, HNA Building on 7 Guoxing Avenue.
Lodestar is the seventh studio album by the English folk musician, Shirley Collins. The album is Collins's first in 38 years, making it one of the longest gaps between studio albums.
Ignatius Masʿūd II was the Syriac Orthodox Patriarch of Ṭur ʿAbdin from 1492. He was also a bishop and writer.
The COVID-19 pandemic in Tunisia is part of the worldwide pandemic of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) caused by SARS-CoV-2. The first case in Tunisia was confirmed on March 2, 2020.

Lou Rinaldi (born 1947) is a Canadian politician. He was a Liberal member of the Legislative Assembly of Ontario, representing Northumberland and Northumberland—Quinte West. Rinaldi was also the mayor of Brighton, Ontario, before entering provincial politics in 2003.

The Western Sahara peace process refers to attempts to resolve the ongoing conflict between the Polisario Front and Morocco over the territory of Western Sahara. The conflict, stemming from the withdrawal of Spain in 1975, has involved armed conflict and civil campaigns. Despite a ceasefire in 1991, tensions persist, marked by protests and demographic changes due to Moroccan settlement schemes.

Soul Power is the debut studio album by American singer Curtis Harding, released May 6, 2014, by Burger Records. It encompasses a wide breadth of genres, though it is primarily inspired by soul music and his mother's gospel singing. The album received generally favorable reviews from music critics.

Kanyaka is an Indian magazine in Malayalam primarily addressing women's issues. It is a fortnightly magazine published by the Mangalam Publications India Pvt. Ltd., Kottayam. Claramma Varghese is Chief Editor and Toshma Biju Varghese is the Managing Editor. Sajil Sreedhar is the Editor-In-Chief. The headquarters is in Mumbai.

The magazine contains features on current affairs, stories, poems, cartoons, interviews of celebrities and uncelebrated common personalities. The magazine is dedicated to the uplifting women in Indian society as well as in the societies of other countries.

Michael Hugh Meacher (4 November 1939 – 21 October 2015) was a British politician who served as a government minister under Harold Wilson, James Callaghan and Tony Blair. A member of the Labour Party, he was Member of Parliament (MP) for Oldham West and Royton, previously Oldham West, from 1970 until his death in 2015. He was also a 9/11 conspiracy theorist.

Before entering politics, Meacher was a lecturer in social administration at the University of Essex and the University of York.

The men's hammer throw at the 2004 Summer Olympics in Athens took place between August 20 and 22 at the Olympic Stadium. Thirty-five athletes from 24 nations competed.

Originally, Adrián Annus won gold, but was disqualified for doping. Koji Murofushi was then awarded gold. Ivan Tsikhan, originally bronze medalist, was also disqualified in 2012 for doping. As a result, Eşref Apak received the bronze medal. The event was the 24th appearance of the men's hammer throw.
*Phlebiella* is a genus of fungi in the order Polyporales.

Pridwen was, according to the 12th-century writer Geoffrey of Monmouth, King Arthur's shield; it was adorned with an image of the Virgin Mary. Geoffrey's description of it draws on earlier Welsh traditions found in Preiddeu Annwfn, Culhwch and Olwen, and the Historia Brittonum. The shield is also named and described by Wace, Layamon, Roger of Wendover and Robert of Gloucester among other medieval writers, and it directly inspired the description of Sir Gawain's shield in Sir Gawain and the Green Knight.

Cuneo railway station is the primary station serving the city of Cuneo, Piedmont, Italy. Opened on November 7, 1937, it is located in Piazzale della Libertà, to the west of the city center. It is operated by Rete Ferroviaria Italiana (RFI) and Centostazioni.

The station is a junction, serving the Fossano–Cuneo, Savigliano–Cuneo, Cuneo–Ventimiglia, and Cuneo–Mondovì lines. It consists of five passenger tracks and eight goods tracks, with a locomotive depot to the south. In 2009, the station was upgraded with a new public information system. Cuneo station sees approximately 3 million passenger movements annually and is served by regional trains to cities including Turin, Savona, Saluzzo, Ventimiglia, and Nice.

Brodie Smith (born in Jacksonville, Florida) is an American Ultimate Disc League player, disc golfer, and trick shot performer. He gained prominence through his frisbee trick shot videos on YouTube, beginning with "Frisbee Trick Shots" in 2011, which has amassed over 5 million views.

Smith played Ultimate at the University of Florida, where he won two National College Championships, in 2006 and 2010. Professionally, he has played in the AUDL for the Indianapolis Alleycats (2011), Chicago Wildfire (2013), Dallas Roughnecks (2016), and Jacksonville Cannons (2017).

Smith's trick shot videos have garnered him national media attention, including appearances on ESPN's SportsCenter Top Plays. He has also collaborated with the YouTube group Dude Perfect. Smith has stated that his goal is to promote awareness for Ultimate as a sport through his trick shot performances.

DWOW (103.5 MHz), branded as K-Lite 103.5, is a radio station broadcasting in Metro Manila, Philippines. Established December 18, 1979, it has gone through several call sign and branding changes, including DWIM, DWCS, DWKX, Heart FM, Max FM, and Wow FM. Currently owned by Advanced Media Broadcasting System, its format is adult contemporary and OPM.

The 17th Arizona State Legislature, consisting of the Arizona State Senate and the Arizona House of Representatives, was constituted in Phoenix from January 1, 1945 to December 31, 1946, during the third of Sidney Preston Osborn's four consecutive terms as Governor of Arizona. The number of senators and house members remained constant at 19 and 58, respectively. The Democrats controlled one hundred percent of the senate. while the Republicans gained a single house seat.

Elections to the Legislative Assembly of the Indian state of Hyderabad were held and Sri Burgula Rama Krishna Rao took oath as First Chief Minister of Hyderabad State on 6 March 1952. 564 candidates competed for the 175 seats in the Assembly. There were 33 two-member constituencies and 109 constituencies single-member constituencies.
E11EVEN Miami is a multilevel nightclub located in downtown Miami. The night club was opened February 5, 2014.

Han Lao Da (born Ann Jong Juan, October 22, 1947) is a Singaporean xiangsheng performer, playwright, and the principal of Han Language Centre. He began composing xiangsheng plays in the 1970s and has since worked to promote the art form in Singapore, publishing several collections of scripts.
Thomas Hourston (1854–1905) was a merchant, farmer and alderman on the Edmonton Town Council from 1896 to 1898.

Milltown (Irish: Baile an Mhuilinn) is a village in County Kildare, Ireland. It is located within the Allen Parish, and is home to the Church of St Brigid, which was originally built in 1817. Milltown GAA, the local Gaelic Athletic Association club, was founded in 1888. In 2016, the village had a population of 344.
Ashley Clements is an American actress. She starred as Lizzie Bennet in the Emmy-winning web series The Lizzie Bennet Diaries.

The Western Sahara peace process refers to the international efforts to resolve the Western Sahara conflict. The conflict has failed so far to result in permanent peace between Morocco and the Polisario Front (including its self declared Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic). The standing issues of the peace process include Sahrawi refugees (specifically Sahrawi refugee camps in Algeria), and human rights in Western Sahara.

Deryan Aktert, chairman of Dicle district for the Justice and Development Party (AKP), was shot by a group of seven or eight people at a gas station owned by him in Dicle on 10 October 2016, around 22:35 TSİ.

Robert Altman (1937 – September 24, 2021) was an American photographer known for his documentation of 1960s counterculture. His work, particularly his photographs of rock musicians, celebrities, and fans, captured the "look and feeling and vitality of the Sixties," according to Jann Wenner, founder of Rolling Stone Magazine.

Altman's photographs have been exhibited at institutions such as Abbey Road Studios, The Beat Museum, Bethel Woods Center for the Arts, the Newseum, and the Georgia Historical Society. His work is part of the permanent collections of The San Francisco Public Library, The Library of Congress, The Smithsonian Institution, The Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, and the Kodak Rock Photography Collection. In 2009, Altman collaborated with Macy's Herald Square, filling its windows with 175 images of his work. He was awarded a Doctor of Arts, Honoris Causa, from Digital Media Arts College in May 2010.

His most well-known collection, "The Sixties: Photographs," portrays the Acid culture, Summer of Love, Rock, and Flower Power generation. Altman's style emphasized capturing raw, natural moments, influenced by the compositional techniques of Ansel Adams, particularly perspective. He died on September 24, 2021, after suffering from esophageal cancer.

The 1952 Hyderabad State Legislative Assembly election was held on March 27, 1952, to elect 175 members to the Hyderabad Legislative Assembly. The Indian National Congress won a majority with 93 seats. Burgula Ramakrishna Rao of INC was elected Chief Minister. The Communist Party of India contested under the cover of the People's Democratic Front.

Boon Island is a barren, rocky island in the Gulf of Maine 6 mi (9.7 km) off the coast of York, Maine. The island, which is approximately 300 ft (91 m) by 700 ft (210 m) at low tide, is the site of Boon Island Light, at 137 ft (42 m) high, it is the tallest lighthouse in New England. Numerous vessels have been wrecked on its rocky shoreline. John Winthrop, the English Puritan lawyer and one of the leading figures in founding the Massachusetts Bay Colony mentions passing Boon Island in the 1600s.

Sanjog (transl. Destiny) is a 1972 Indian Hindi-language drama film directed by S. S. Balan. The film stars Mala Sinha, Amitabh Bachchan and Aruna Irani in the lead roles. It is a remake of the Tamil film Iru Kodugal directed by K. Balachander.

The Ruben M. Benjamin House is a house in Bloomington, Illinois. It is a two-story rectangular building, styled in the Classical Revival architectural motif. It was built in 1856 John L. Routt, who would become the first governor of Colorado. Ruben M. Benjamin, an attorney known for litigation relating to railroad regulation, lived in the home for more than 60 years. The United States National Register of Historic Places added the Ruben M. Benjamin House in August 1978.

A debug port is a chip-level diagnostic interface (akin to a computer port) included in an integrated circuit to aid design, fabrication, development, bootstrapping, configuration, debugging, and post-sale in-system programming. In general terms, a debug port is not necessary for end-use function and is often hidden or disabled in finished products.

When constituted in a high availability or safety critical system, a debug port may continue to operate as a system monitor for integrity validation in the finished product. In low-cost or high-volume electronics, debug ports are sometimes left enabled to avoid costs of design changes, and can be used by modders and hackers to gain control of the device and/or access to extra functionality.

Due to their high complexity, debug ports are nearly universal in microprocessors (CPUs) and microcontrollers (MCUs). These are discussed below.
**Richard Sukuta-Pasu** (born 24 June 1990) is a German professional footballer who plays as a forward for Vejle Boldklub.
Marguerite Louppe (1902–1989) was a French painter known for her exploration of mathematical and abstract artistic trends, intimate scenes of Parisian life, still lifes, and landscapes.

Ajantrik (known internationally as The Unmechanical, The Mechanical Man or The Pathetic Fallacy) is a 1958 Indian Bengali film written and directed by revered parallel filmmaker Ritwik Ghatak. The film is adapted from a Bengali short story of the same name written by Subodh Ghosh.

A comedy-drama film, Ajantrik is one of the earliest Indian films to portray an inanimate object, in this case an automobile, as a character in the story. It achieves this through the use of sounds recorded post-production to emphasize the car's bodily functions and movements.

The film was considered for a special entry in the Venice Film Festival in 1959.

Deryan Aktert, chairman of Dicle district, was assassinated on October 10, 2016, in Dicle, Turkey, by the People's Defence Forces (HPG), the armed wing of the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK). Aktert had been threatened and targeted in prior assassination attempts.

Sendle is an Australian-based company which provides courier services within the country, focusing primarily on small to medium-sized businesses and online retailers. It claims to be the first courier company in Australia to offer fully carbon neutral services. While its delivery services are worldwide - with the exception of Russia and Belarus - its operational staff are based in Australia, New Zealand, the Philippines and the United States.

Whitland Abbey (Lang-cy or simply Y Tŷ Gwyn ar Daf; Latin, Albalanda) was a country house and Cistercian abbey in the parish of Llangan, in what was the hundred of Narbeth, Carmarthenshire, Wales. The town which grew up nearby is now named Whitland after it. It was widely known as Ty Gwyn ar Daf, meaning White House on the Taf, in reference to the country house originally built here before it became a monastic settlement which was known under that name. It is most associated with being the place where Hywel Dda drew up his laws around 940. It functioned as a Cistercian monastery between the 12th and 16th centuries.

Benjamin Brackett Downing (born September 11, 1981) is an American politician and Democratic former member of the Massachusetts State Senate. He represented the Berkshire, Hampshire and Franklin district from 2007 to 2013, before being redistricted to the Berkshire, Hampshire, Franklin, and Hampden district, which he represented until 2017.

*Sanjog* is a 1972 Indian Hindi-language film, directed by S. S. Balan and produced by Ayaz Ali Shaikh. The film stars Mala Sinha, Amitabh Bachchan, and Aruna Irani. The film is about Mohan and Asha who get married but are separated by Mohan's mother.
Leon Mike (born 4 September 1981) is an English former footballer who played as a striker. He is the current manager of Burscough.

Saifuddin Azizi (Lang-ug; 12 March 1915 – 24 November 2003), also known as Seypidin Azizi, Saif al-Dīn ʿAzīz, Saifuding Aizezi and Saifuding, was the first chairman of the Xinjiang Uyghur Autonomous Region of the People's Republic of China.

Han Lao Da (Chinese: 韩劳达; pinyin: Hán Láodá) is a Singaporean playwright, as well as founder and principal of Han Language Centre. Han received the Cultural Medallion for his contributions in the Singaporean drama scene, and is also recognised for his xiangsheng contributions in Singapore.

Zoya Yunoevna Semenduyeva (1929–2020) was a Soviet and Israeli poet. Writing in the Mountain Jewish language (Juhuri), she published numerous collections of poetry, exploring themes of love, friendship, and war. She immigrated to Israel in 1992.

Eugène-René Poubelle (15 April 1831 – 15 July 1907) was a French lawyer and diplomat who introduced waste containers to Paris and made their use compulsory. This introduction was so innovative at the time that Poubelle's surname became synonymous with waste bins (la poubelle) and remains the most common French word for a bin.

The Offer in Compromise (OIC) program, in the United States, is an Internal Revenue Service (IRS) program under 26 U.S.C. § 7122, which allows qualified individuals with an unpaid tax debt to negotiate a settled amount that is less than the total owed to clear the debt. A taxpayer uses the checklist in the Form 656, OIC package to determine if the taxpayer is eligible for the program. The objective of the OIC program is to accept a compromise when acceptance is in the best interests of both the taxpayer and the government, and promotes voluntary compliance with all future payment and filing requirements.

Korzkiew Castle is a Romanesque castle in Korzkiew, Lesser Poland Voivodeship, Poland. Built in the 14th century, it initially served as a defensive and residential tower. Throughout its history, the castle underwent several rebuilds and changed ownership multiple times. Since 1997, the castle has been privately owned and operates as a hotel.

Ninnes is a locality at the northeastern corner of Yorke Peninsula and western side of the Mid North of South Australia. It lies where the Upper Yorke Road from Kulpara to Bute is crossed by the road from Paskeville to Lochiel. The dominant industry is broadacre grain and sheep farming.

*Youngblood* is the third studio album by Australian pop rock band 5 Seconds of Summer, released on June 15, 2018. The album marked a shift towards a pop sound, incorporating elements of dance-pop and influences from bands like The 1975 and Imagine Dragons.

Preceded by the single "Want You Back", the album spawned the title track "Youngblood", which achieved significant commercial success, topping charts worldwide and earning numerous certifications. "Valentine" and "Lie to Me" were also released as singles. Prior to the album's release, the band embarked on the 5SOS III Tour, followed by the Meet You There Tour to promote the album.

*Youngblood* debuted at number one in Australia and the United States, making 5 Seconds of Summer the first Australian act with three number-one albums in the US. The album received mixed reviews from critics, with some praising the band's evolution while others felt it lacked the energy of their earlier work. *Youngblood* won the ARIA Award for Best Group in 2018. The album's title track was named Australia's 2018 song of the year and has been streamed over 1 billion times.

DXWT (92.3 FM), branded as 92.3 Wild FM, is a radio station broadcasting in Metro Davao, Philippines. Owned by UM Broadcasting Network, it airs a contemporary MOR format with dance and OPM music in Cebuano, Filipino, and English. Established in 1949 as DXMC on AM, it transitioned to FM in 1988, becoming Wild FM.

David Upthegrove (born May 3, 1971) is an American politician. He is a member of the King County Council, representing the 5th district since 2014. A member of the Democratic Party, he was a member of the Washington House of Representatives, representing the 33rd district from 2002 to 2013.
Fitchville Township is one of the nineteen townships of Huron County, Ohio, United States. The population was 1,056 at the 2010 census.

Cuneo railway station, or Cuneo Altipiano railway station (Lang-it or Stazione di Cuneo Altipiano), is the main station serving the city and comune of Cuneo, in the Piedmont region, northwestern Italy. Opened in 1937, it is the junction of the Fossano–Cuneo, Savigliano–Cuneo, Cuneo–Ventimiglia and Cuneo–Mondovì railways.

The station is currently managed by Rete Ferroviaria Italiana (RFI). However, the commercial area of the passenger building is managed by Centostazioni. Train services are operated by Trenitalia. Each of these companies is a subsidiary of Ferrovie dello Stato (FS), Italy's state-owned rail company.
