The other station in the city, Cuneo Gesso, forms part of the Cuneo–Mondovì railway, and is not far from the hamlet of Borgo San Giuseppe (formerly Borgo Gesso).
Rikuchū-Noda Station (陸中野田駅, Rikuchū-Noda-eki) is a railway station on the Rias Line in the village of Noda, Iwate, Japan, operated by the third-sector railway operator Sanriku Railway.

Tō Tsuneyori (東 常縁, 1401 – 1484) was a Japanese samurai lord and poet. He was the lord of Shinowaki Castle in Mino Province. He studied waka poetry and founded Kōkin Denju, a school of Kokin Wakashū interpretation. He is known as the author of *Tōyashū kikigaki*, *Shin-Kokin Wakashū kikigaki*, and *Tōyashū shūda*.

*The Postcard Killings* is a 2020 American crime film directed by Danis Tanović, based on the novel *The Postcard Killers* by Liza Marklund and James Patterson. It stars Jeffrey Dean Morgan as a New York detective investigating the murder of his daughter while she was on her honeymoon in London.
HD 37974 (or R 126) a variable B[e] hypergiant in the Large Magellanic Cloud. It is surrounded by an unexpected dust disk.

Greeny is a 1959 Gibson Les Paul Standard, named after its original owner Peter Green. He used it during his time in John Mayall & the Bluesbreakers and Fleetwood Mac, before selling it to Gary Moore in the early 1970s. Moore used the guitar throughout his career, both as a solo artist and in bands, such as Thin Lizzy. Due to financial troubles, he was forced to sell it in 2006, after which it passed through several private collectors and guitar dealers. In 2014, Greeny was acquired by Metallica guitarist Kirk Hammett, who has since used it both in the studio and during live performances.

The guitar is known for its unique out-of-phase tone, which is the result of a reversed neck pickup. This sound has become highly sought-after amongst guitar players, so much so that flipping the neck pickup has been affectionately referred to as "the Peter Green mod". The Gibson Custom Shop has produced two limited runs of Greeny replicas in 2010 and 2022, repectively. Gibson also released the Gary Moore Les Paul Standard in 2013, which included many of the same features as Greeny.

2002 Euler is a stony background asteroid from the inner regions of the asteroid belt, approximately 17 kilometers (11 miles) in diameter. It was discovered on 29 August 1973, by Russian astronomer Tamara Smirnova at the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory in Nauchnyj, and assigned the prov. designation 1973 QQ1. It was named after Swiss mathematician Leonhard Euler.

The men's hammer throw competition at the 2004 Summer Olympics in Athens was held at the Olympic Stadium on 20–22 August. There were 35 competitors from 24 nations. The event was marred by doping-related disqualifications, with the original first- and third-place athletes being removed. After those disqualifications, the event was won by Koji Murofushi of Japan, the nation's first medal in the event. No silver medal was awarded. Eşref Apak of Turkey received bronze, Turkey's first medal in the event as well.
Robert Sithole (1945 – 7 June 2006), was a South African musician.

*Shade* is a 2003 American neo-noir crime thriller film directed by Damian Nieman and starring Stuart Townsend, Gabriel Byrne, and Thandiwe Newton. The plot revolves around a group of hustlers in the world of underground poker, who attempt to con a legendary card player known as "The Dean." The film had a limited theatrical release in 2004.
Hugh Cecil Lowther, 5th Earl of Lonsdale, KG, GCVO, DL (25 January 1857–13 April 1944) was an English peer and sportsman.

Tō Tsuneyori (東 常縁, 1401 - 1484) was a samurai lord and poet of the mid-Muromachi period and early Sengoku period. He was the lord of Shinowaki Castle in Mino Province and served as Governor of Shimotsuke Province. He is known as the founder of Kōkin Denju poetic school. He held the court rank of Junior Fifth Rank. He was also known as Tōyashū.

William Forsythe (born June 7, 1955) is an American actor. He began his career with minor film roles and guest appearances in television shows such as *CHiPs*, *Hill Street Blues*, and *T. J. Hooker*. Forsythe has appeared in films such as *Once Upon a Time in America* (1984), *Raising Arizona* (1987), *Dick Tracy* (1990), and *Out for Justice* (1991). He also played Sammy "The Bull" Gravano in *Gotti* (1996) and John Wayne Gacy in *Dear Mr. Gacy* (2010).
Roberto Poljak (1932–2024) was an Argentine-American biophysicist and professor known for determining the 3-D structure of an antibody.

The Moldavian Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic (MASSR) was an autonomous republic of the Ukrainian SSR from 1924 to 1940. It was established on October 12, 1924, from parts of the Odessa and Podolian Governorates, with the proclaimed capital of Chișinău ("temporarily occupied"), though the actual capital was Balta (1924–1929) and later Tiraspol (1929–1940).

The MASSR was created partly to promote Communist ideas to neighboring Bessarabia (then part of Romania) and potentially spark a revolution there. The republic underwent rapid industrialization and collectivization, accompanied by migration from other Soviet republics. The MASSR experienced famines in 1925, 1932, and 1933, the latter as part of the Holodomor.

The MASSR had a mixed population, with Moldavians comprising less than one-third. The government promoted the idea that the Moldavian language was distinct from Romanian, using a reformed Cyrillic script. In 1940, following a Soviet ultimatum to Romania, the MASSR was dissolved, and its territory was combined with Bessarabia to form the Moldavian Soviet Socialist Republic.

Acalymma vittatum, the striped cucumber beetle, is a beetle of the family Chrysomelidae and a serious pest of cucurbit crops in both larval and adult stages. It is distributed from eastern North America to the Rocky Mountains. It is replaced in the west by Acalymma trivittatum, a duller species often with greyish or pale white elytra rather than yellow.
South Bedfordshire was, from 1974 to 2009, a non-metropolitan district of Bedfordshire, in the East of England. Its main towns were Dunstable, Houghton Regis and Leighton Buzzard.

Eastern Macedonia and Thrace (Lang-el, IPA-el) is one of the thirteen administrative regions of Greece. It consists of the northeastern parts of the country, comprising the eastern part of the region of Macedonia along with the region of Western Thrace, and the islands of Thasos and Samothrace.

Neodiprion abietis, commonly known as the balsam fir sawfly, is a species of insect in the family Diprionidae. It is found in North America from Canada to northern Mexico and is phytophagous, feeding on the needles of coniferous trees.

*Neodiprion abietis*, also known as the balsam fir sawfly, is a species of sawfly in the family Diprionidae. The larvae feed on fir, spruce, and larch trees. Outbreaks of *N. abietis* can reduce tree growth and cause tree mortality.

Ranpak Holdings Corp. is a publicly traded company (NYSE: PACK) specializing in recyclable packaging solutions and machinery. Founded in 1972 in Painesville, Ohio, by Raymond Q. Armington and George R. Johnson, Ranpak originated from a 1970 patent for a machine that converted kraft paper into cushioning material. The company serves markets across North and South America, Europe, the Middle East, East and Southeast Asia, and Australasia. Omar Asali is the current CEO. In 2021, Fast Company recognized Ranpak as a top innovator in logistics for its paper-based alternatives to traditional shipping materials.

Acacia parramattensis, commonly known as Parramatta wattle, is a tree of the family Fabaceae native to the Blue Mountains and surrounding regions of New South Wales. It is a tall shrub or tree to about 15 m (49 ft) in height with phyllodes (flattened leaf stalks) instead of true leaves. These are finely divided bipinnate. The yellow flowers appear over summer. It generally grows in woodland or dry sclerophyll forest on alluvial or shale-based soils, generally with some clay content.

A fast-growing plant, it regenerates after bushfire by seed or suckering and can quickly colonise disturbed areas. Likewise it adapts readily to cultivation and is used in revegetation projects.
Oliver County is a county located in the U.S. state of North Dakota. As of the 2020 census, the population was 1,877. Its county seat and only city is Center.

Pakistan has bilateral and multilateral trade agreements with many nations and international organizations. It is a member of the World Trade Organization, part of the South Asian Free Trade Area agreement and the China–Pakistan Free Trade Agreement. Fluctuating world demand for its exports, domestic political uncertainty, and the impact of occasional droughts on its agricultural production have all contributed to variability in Pakistan's trade deficit. The trade deficit for the fiscal year 2013/14 is $7.743 billion, exports are $10.367 billion in July–November 2013 and imports are $18.110 billion.

Pakistan's exports continue to be dominated by manpower export in the subcontinent, cotton textiles and apparel. Imports include petroleum and petroleum products, chemicals, fertilizer, capital goods, industrial raw materials, and consumer products.

On 12 December 2013, the European Union granted GSP Plus status to Pakistan until 2017, which enabled it to export 20% of its good with 0 tariff and 70 percent at preferential rates to the EU market. This status was given after the European Parliament passed the resolution by 406-186 votes.

Rod Diridon Sr. (born February 8, 1939) is an American retired elected official and non-profit leader. He is known for his work in Silicon Valley transportation. He chaired the Santa Clara County Board of Supervisors and the California High-Speed Rail Authority. He is considered "the father of modern transit service" in Silicon Valley. San Jose Diridon Train Station is named in his honor. He is the founding executive director emeritus of the Mineta Transportation Institute (MTI).

*Icon* is a 2010 compilation album by Trisha Yearwood, released by MCA Nashville. It contains 12 previously-recorded hit singles by Yearwood between 1991 and 2001, produced by Garth Fundis, Tony Brown, Mark Wright, Harry Stinson, and Yearwood herself. The album reached number 69 on the *Billboard* Top Country Albums chart. Stephen Thomas Erlewine of Allmusic gave the release four out of five stars.

Stephen Langton (c. 1150 – 9 July 1228) was an English Cardinal of the Roman Catholic Church and Archbishop of Canterbury between 1207 and his death in 1228. The dispute between King John of England and Pope Innocent III over his election was a major factor in the crisis which produced Magna Carta in 1215. Cardinal Langton is also credited with having divided the Bible into the standard modern arrangement of chapters used today.

Seneca Falls is a hamlet and census-designated place in Seneca County, New York, United States. The population was 6,681 at the 2010 census. The 2020 census population of Seneca Falls CDP was 6,809. The hamlet is in the Town of Seneca Falls, east of Geneva. It was an incorporated village from 1831 to 2011.

Finger Lakes Regional Airport (0G7) is south of the hamlet.

Seneca Falls was the site of the Seneca Falls Convention, a foundational event in the Women's Rights Movement. The town is believed by some to have been the inspiration for the fictional town of "Bedford Falls, N.Y." portrayed in filmmaker Frank Capra's classic 1940s film "It's a Wonderful Life".
Paulus Vallius (born in Rome) was a lecturer and Thomist philosopher whose notes were used by Galileo.
Linda Gottlieb is an American television writer and film producer.

DXWT (92.3 FM), broadcasting as 92.3 Wild FM, is a radio station owned and operated by the UM Broadcasting Network. It serves as the flagship station of the Wild FM network. The station's studio is located at the UMBN Media Center, C. Bangoy cor. Palma Gil St., Davao City, and its transmitter is located along Broadcast Ave., Shrine Hills, Matina, Davao City.
Neopleurotomoides rufoapicata is a species of sea snail, a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Raphitomidae.

Fatima Bhutto (born May 29, 1982) is a Pakistani writer and columnist. Born in Kabul, Afghanistan, she is the granddaughter of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto and niece of Benazir Bhutto. She received her education at Barnard College of Columbia University and SOAS, University of London.

Bhutto is the author of several books, including *Whispers of The Desert*, *8.50 a.m. 8 October 2005*, the family memoir *Songs of Blood and Sword*, and the novels *The Shadow Of The Crescent Moon* and *The Runaways*. Her work often explores themes of politics, identity, and social justice.

Despite speculation following her aunt's assassination, Bhutto has chosen to remain outside of electoral politics, focusing instead on activism and writing. She identifies as a secularist and a cultural Muslim.

Samira Ćosović is a Serbian language and literature teacher from Brodarevo, Serbia, who has served in the Prijepolje municipal assembly since 2008. She was elected to the National Assembly of Serbia in the 2020 Serbian parliamentary election. Ćosović resigned from the National Assembly when she was appointed as deputy mayor of Prijepolje in December 2021.

The Newmarket Viaduct was opened on 3 September 1966 as New Zealand's first pair of balanced cantilever bridges. Due to earthquake vulnerability and increasing traffic, it was replaced between 2007 and 2013.
Paul De Keersmaeker (born 1929) is a Belgian politician, businessman, and honorary chairman of Interbrew and KBC Bank.
Stephen James White (born 2 January 1959) is an English former professional footballer and manager.
Rose Venkatesan is the first Indian talk show host based in Chennai, Tamil Nadu. She is a trans woman, and has been active in many fields like television and radio.

Hotel Kazakhstan (Lang-kk, Lang-ru) is the third-tallest building in the city of Almaty, Kazakhstan. It is 102 m (335 ft) tall, with 26 floors. It is situated in the southern center of Almaty. It is located on Dostyq Avenue, a major road in the east part of the city which runs all the way from Medeo to Panfilov Park, in the north part of town.

The total area is 44887.5 sq. meters.

The building was erected in 1977. It is constructed to stand an earthquake that measures 9.0 on the Richter scale. It is a famous landmark all over Almaty, and serves as a symbol of the city. It is also the eighth-tallest building in all Kazakhstan.

In October 2018, architectural lighting was installed on the hotel building.

The "Friendship" public garden adjoins the hotel territory, where the Camels panel, a monument to Shamshi Kaldayakov and a fountain are located. Convenient access to the embankment of the Malaya Almatinka River has been arranged.

A debug port on a microprocessor or microcontroller (MCU) is an interface used for debugging and configuring the device. Modern microprocessors are exceptionally complex, containing billions of transistors. MCUs, evolving into systems on a chip (SoCs), often rely on debug ports for initial configuration, such as setting up the clock tree or loading the first software program after manufacturing, especially when internal flash memory and EEPROM-based configuration fuses are involved.

Some MCUs feature integrated USB peripherals with device firmware upgrade (DFU) capabilities in factory ROM, offering an alternative to specialized debug ports like JTAG. DFU allows for recovery from bad firmware loads by booting from internal factory ROM, providing a programming interface through the built-in USB controller. However, this software-based approach can be fragile when debugging the very software that implements the debug interface.

Joseph Buckland (born 27 October 1992) is a British ice dancer. He has competed with multiple partners, including Olivia Smart, Molly Lanaghan, and Robynne Tweedale. With Smart, he won the 2015 British national title. His brother is Nicholas Buckland, who also competes in ice dancing.
Thomas Hourston (February 12, 1854 – April 11, 1905) was a politician from Alberta, Canada and a municipal councillor in Edmonton.

An air pump is a pump for pushing air. Examples include a bicycle pump, pumps that are used to aerate an aquarium or a pond via an airstone; a gas compressor used to power a pneumatic tool, air horn or pipe organ; a bellows used to encourage a fire; a vacuum cleaner and a vacuum pump. All air pumps contain a part that moves (vane, piston, impeller, diaphragm etc.) which drives the flow of air. When the air gets moved, an area of low pressure gets created which fills up with more air.

Pumps and compressors use very similar mechanisms, and basically perform the same action, but in different fluid regimes. At some point there is a crossover point in terminology, but here are some stereotypes:

• Compressors operate on compressible fluids, typically gases. Pumps operate on fluids, typically liquids, approximated as in-compressible.

• Compressors are intended to develop a very high pressure rise against a closed system; pumps are designed to develop relatively little pressure against a free-flowing system with minimal back-pressure.

• Pumps are often used in continuous-flow operation, while many lower-end compressors must have intermittent duty cycles.

• Compressors usually have a feedback sensor to shut off when they reach a desired pressure; pumps have a fixed design and operate freely across their performance curve as conditions change

Shigeichi Nagano (1925–2019) was a Japanese photographer known for his street photography, particularly his humorous depictions of Tokyo's salarymen during Japan's economic boom of the 1960s. He also worked in film and television after working on Tokyo Olympiad.

Immune Therapy Holdings is a privately held Swedish research and development company founded in 2005. It focuses on immunotherapy, including its Tailored Leukapheresis (TLA) treatment. TLA is an apheresis immunotherapy for selective removal of disease-causing pro-inflammatory cells extracorporeally.

Nutrisystem is a commercial provider of weight loss products and services headquartered in Fort Washington, Pennsylvania. Nutrisystem's initial product in 1972 was a liquid protein diet, but the company changed its offering after Slim-Fast came to prominence in that market. The company originally offered weight loss counseling and products in brick and mortar centers, but in 1999, Nutrisystem moved to a direct-to-consumer business model, selling its products and programs on the Internet and by telephone.

The company launched its mobile platform in 2010. WebDiet, Inc., a Silicon Valley startup, accused Nutrisystem of stealing their technology, but the case was dismissed in May, 2014.

In 2021, Stephen Mikulak was named President of Nutrisystem.

In 2015, the firm acquired the South Beach Diet brand. In December 2018, Tivity Health announced that it would acquire the Nutrisystem brand. On October 19, 2020, Kainos Capital acquired the brand from Tivity Health.

Select Registry, Distinguished Inns of North America, is a non-profit association of more than 300 country inns, B&Bs and boutique hotels throughout the United States. Founded in 1972, the association is based in Greensboro, Georgia, United States and led by CEO Mark Reichle.

Rafe Mazzeo (born 1961) is an American mathematician and professor at Stanford University. His research has been published in prestigious journals and cited thousands of times. He is a fellow of the American Mathematical Society and a member of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences.

Hualing Nieh Engle (born 11 January 1925), née Nieh Hua-ling (Chinese: 聶華苓; pinyin: Niè Huálíng; Wade–Giles: Nieh4 Hua2-ling2), is a Chinese novelist, fiction writer, and poet. She is a professor emerita at the University of Iowa.

The Satanita AC 59 is an English contract law case, decided in the Court of Appeal, which concerned the formation of a contract. It is notable because it stands as an example of a case which does not fit the typical pattern of offer and acceptance that English law purports to require to find agreement.

Capsule hotel (Lang-ja), also known in the Western world as a pod hotel, is a type of hotel developed in Japan that features many small bed-sized rooms known as capsules. Capsule hotels provide cheap, basic overnight accommodation for guests who do not require or who cannot afford larger, more expensive rooms offered by more conventional hotels.

The first capsule hotel in the world opened in 1979 and was the Capsule Inn Osaka, located in the Umeda district of Osaka, Japan and designed by Kisho Kurokawa. From there, it spread to other cities within Japan. Since then, the concept has further spread to various other territories, including Belgium, China, Hong Kong, Iceland, India, Indonesia, Poland and Canada.

Bugando Medical Centre (BMC) is a referral, research, and teaching hospital in Mwanza, Tanzania. Opened in 1971, it has 950 beds and is affiliated with the Catholic University of Health and Allied Sciences.

Spirito DiVino is the seventh studio album by the Italian blues rock singer-songwriter Zucchero Fornaciari, released on 27 May 1995 by Polydor Records. The album was also released in partial Spanish language edition, and English edition titled Spirito DiVino: Stray Cat in a Mad Dog City.

David Peter Ryan (born 5 January 1957) is an English former professional footballer who played as a goalkeeper. He played in the English Football League for Port Vale in 1976, on loan from Manchester United. He later played non-League football for Southport, Northwich Victoria, Hyde United and Chorley.

*Twentieth Century* is a studio album by the American country band Alabama, released on June 15, 1999, through RCA Nashville. The album was produced by Alabama and Don Cook at Soundshop Recording Studios in Nashville, Tennessee.

Critical reception to *Twentieth Century* was mixed. Stephen Thomas Erlewine of Allmusic gave the album 2 out of 5 stars, noting its technical proficiency but criticizing its lack of distinctiveness. He cited "Twentieth Century", "Mist of Desire", "Life's Too Short to Love This Fast", and "God Must Have Spent a Little More Time on You" as the album's strongest tracks, but found them largely indistinguishable from the rest of the album. Mark Herndon, the band's drummer at the time, is not credited on the album.
The Newmarket Viaduct is a seven-lane state highway viaduct in Auckland, New Zealand. The 700 m long viaduct, which is up to 20m high, carries the Southern Motorway over the Newmarket suburb.
Lincoln is a census-designated place in Lewis and Clark County, Montana, United States. The population was 998 at the 2020 census.

Zoya Yunoevna Semenduyeva (Lang-ru; Lang-he; born 20 October 1929 – 9 April 2020) was a Soviet and Israeli poet. She wrote in a language of the Mountain Jew (Juhuri). She was a member of Dagestan Writers' Union.

Arkady Adamovich Brish (May 14, 1917 – March 19, 2016) was born in Minsk, in what was then the Russian Empire. He graduated with a degree in Physics from Belarusian State University in 1940. During World War II, he joined an underground group resisting Nazi troops.

Edwin "Honest Ed" Mirvish, OC CBE OOnt (July 24, 1914 – July 11, 2007) was an American-Canadian businessman, philanthropist and theatrical impresario who lived in Toronto, Ontario. He is known for his flagship business, Honest Ed's, a landmark discount store in downtown Toronto, and as a patron of the arts, instrumental in revitalizing the theatre scene in Toronto.

José Antonio Páez (1790–1873) was a Venezuelan leader who fought for independence from Spain and was president of Venezuela for three terms. Rising through military ranks, he earned nicknames like "El Centauro de los Llanos" for his cavalry skills. Páez played a crucial role in the Battle of Carabobo (1821), securing Venezuelan independence.

In 1826, Páez led "La Cosiata," a movement asserting Venezuelan autonomy within Gran Colombia. In 1830, he declared Venezuela's independence and became president. Páez dominated Venezuelan politics from 1830 to 1847, either directly as president or through puppet leaders. Exiled twice, he briefly returned to power in 1861. Páez spent his final years in New York City, where he died.

Theresa Park is an American literary agent and producer. After working as a corporate attorney, she became a literary agent in 1994, founding Park Literary and Media in 2005. In 2019, she co-founded Park & Fine Literary and Media. Park is also the president of Per Capita Productions, which is producing the film *After Yang* and the television series *The Expatriates* and *ROAR*.

Alba Vergés i Bosch (born September 3, 1978) is a Spanish politician from Catalonia. She is a member of the Republican Left of Catalonia. She has been the Acting President, as well as First Vice President, of the Parliament of Catalonia. She previously served as the Minister of Health.

Lu Rongjing (Chinese: 卢荣景; born August 1933) is a Chinese politician who served as governor of Anhui from 1988 to 1989, party secretary of Anhui from 1988 to 1996, and chairman of the Anhui Provincial Committee of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference from 1996 to 2000.

He was a delegate to the 7th National People's Congress. He was a member of the Standing Committee of the 9th and 10th Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference. He was a member of the 13th, 14th and 15th Central Committee of the Chinese Communist Party.
Michael Klein (10 October 1959 – 2 February 1993) was a Romanian footballer who played as a left back.

The National Democratic Party (NDP) was a right wing political party that operated in the United Kingdom during the 1960s and 1970s. The NDP sought to position itself as an early rival to the National Front although ultimately it failed to challenge the position of this group.

Rafe Roys Mazzeo (born 1961) is an American mathematician working in differential geometry, microlocal analysis, and partial differential equations. He is currently a professor of mathematics and the department chair at Stanford University.

Twentieth Century is the eighteenth studio album by American country music band Alabama, released in 1999 by RCA Records. It produced the singles "(God Must Have Spent) A Little More Time on You", a collaboration with 'N Sync, "Small Stuff", "We Made Love" and "Twentieth Century", which respectively reached numbers 3, 24, 63, and 51 on the Hot Country Songs charts. In addition, "(God Must Have Spent) A Little More Time on You" was the band's last top ten hit on the country charts.

"(God Must Have Spent) A Little More Time On You" was originally recorded by 'N Sync in 1998 on their first album *NSYNC.

The album peaked at No. 5 on Billboard Country Albums chart and No. 51 on the Billboard 200.

Merewether is a suburb of Newcastle, New South Wales, Australia, located 3 km southwest of Newcastle's central business district. Originally part of the Burwood Estate, it was named after Edward Christopher Merewether. The suburb is known for its beaches, including Merewether Beach, home to the Merewether Ocean Baths.

Yaser Abdel Said (Lang-ar; born January 27, 1957) is an Egyptian-American former taxi driver and convicted murderer. For 12 years, Said evaded arrest for the January 1, 2008, fatal shootings of his two daughters, Amina (18) and Sarah (17). Their bodies were found in his abandoned taxi cab in Irving, Texas, on the property of the Omni Mandalay Hotel (now the Omni Las Colinas Hotel).

Said disappeared after the killings. He also remained a fugitive from law enforcement for 12 years, with six of those years being on the FBI Ten Most Wanted Fugitives List for the fatal shootings.

Said, who was very controlling over his family, felt that his daughters Amina and Sarah dishonored the family. On January 1, 2008, he lured them into his taxi cab on the pretense of taking them out to eat, where instead, he fatally shot them.

Said was captured on August 26, 2020 in the town of Justin, Texas. His son, Islam, and Said's brother, Yassein, were both arrested in Euless, Texas, for aiding a fugitive. Said was taken into federal custody by the FBI, and was then transferred to Dallas County where he was held until his trial, which commenced in August 2022. Said was found guilty on August 9, after a week-long trial.

Alice's Restaurant is a 1969 American comedy film directed by Arthur Penn. It is an adaptation of the 1967 folk song "Alice's Restaurant Massacree", originally written and sung by Arlo Guthrie. The film stars Guthrie as himself, with Pat Quinn as Alice Brock and James Broderick as Ray Brock. Penn, who resided in the story's setting of Stockbridge, Massachusetts, co-wrote the screenplay in 1967 with Venable Herndon after hearing the song, shortly after directing Bonnie & Clyde.

Alice's Restaurant was released on August 19, 1969, a few days after Guthrie appeared at the Woodstock Festival. A soundtrack album for the film was also released by United Artists Records. The soundtrack includes a studio version of the title song, which was originally divided into two parts (one for each album side); a 1998 CD reissue on the Rykodisc label presents this version of the song in full, and adds several bonus tracks to the original LP.
Wigstan (died c.840 AD), also known as Saint Wystan, was the son of Wigmund of Mercia and Ælfflæd, daughter of King Ceolwulf I of Mercia.

A leg is a weight-bearing and locomotive anatomical structure, usually having a columnar shape. During locomotion, legs function as "extensible struts". The combination of movements at all joints can be modeled as a single, linear element capable of changing length and rotating about an omnidirectional "hip" joint.

As an anatomical animal structure it is used for locomotion. The distal end is often modified to distribute force (such as a foot). Most animals have an even number of legs.

As a component of furniture, it is used for the economy of materials needed to provide the support for the useful surface such as the table top or chair seat.

W. & J. Sloane was a luxury furniture and rug store in New York City that catered to the prominent, including the White House and the Breakers, and wealthy, including the Rockefeller, Whitney, and Vanderbilt families.

After a long period of prosperity and prominence, the firm went through a short period of decline and was forced to file for bankruptcy in 1985.
The Fury of the Aquabats! is the second studio album by American rock band the Aquabats, released on October 28, 1997 by Goldenvoice Records and Time Bomb Recordings.
Karlo Matković (born 30 March 2001) is a Croatian professional basketball player for Cedevita Olimpija of the ABA League. He represents the Croatia national team internationally.

Climax is a village in Kalamazoo County, Michigan, United States. The population was 712 at the 2020 census. The village is located within Climax Township. It was incorporated in 1899. Climax was the first community in Michigan to receive free rural mail service in 1896.
Richard Sukuta-Pasu (born 24 June 1990) is a German professional footballer who plays as a forward for Vejle Boldklub.
Nicholas Nichols Cox (January 6, 1837 – May 2, 1912) was an American politician and a member of the United States House of Representatives for the Tennessee's 7th congressional district.

"Ghost Town" is a song by the American rock band Cheap Trick, released in 1988 as the second single from their tenth studio album *Lap of Luxury*. It was written by Diane Warren and Rick Nielsen, and produced by Richie Zito. The song's music video, directed by Nick Morris, received significant airplay on MTV. Upon release, "Ghost Town" received positive reviews, with critics praising Robin Zander's vocals and the song's blend of rock and power pop elements. It was also noted for its resemblance to Electric Light Orchestra (ELO).
"Christians, awake, salute the happy morn" is an English Christmas hymn on a text by John Byrom. It is usually sung to the tune "Yorkshire" by John Wainright.

In object-oriented programming, method cascading is a syntax that allows multiple methods to be called sequentially on the same object, eliminating the need to repeatedly reference the object. This is syntactic sugar that is particularly useful in fluent interfaces. Method cascading differs from method chaining in that it evaluates to the left object, while method chaining evaluates to the right object. Smalltalk and Dart are two languages that implement cascades. Visual Basic uses the `With` statement to achieve a similar result.

The Fairchild 9440 MICROFLAME, also known as the F9440 and μFLAME, was a 16-bit microprocessor introduced by Fairchild Semiconductor in 1977. The 9440 implemented the Data General Nova 2's instruction set in a single-chip 40-pin DIP. The name "MICROFLAME" was part of a wider branding exercise called "FIRE", which was a development software system.

An updated version, the 9445, was announced in 1978 but did not reach market until late 1981. By this time the 16-bit designs were being surpassed by 32-bit designs and hybrids like the Motorola 68000, and Fairchild began turning their attention to their own 32-bit Fairchild Clipper design. The underlying core of the 9445 was also used to implement the 9450, which used new microcode to implement the MIL-STD-1750A instruction set.

The 9440 and 9445 were subject to constant lawsuits from Data General (DG) that dragged on both companies. DG finally settled all ongoing litigation in September 1986 by paying Fairchild over $52 million. Fairchild was purchased and sold several times during this period, ending up at National Semiconductor in 1987. National ended production of the 9445, leaving several users in the lurch. A final version, the IDC9445, was introduced to fill this need.

2002 Euler, provisional designation 1973 QQ1, is a main-belt asteroid discovered on 29 August 1973, by T. Smirnova at the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory. It orbits the Sun every 3.76 years. The asteroid was named after Swiss mathematician Leonhard Euler (1707–1783), who contributed to theories regarding the motion of the Moon.

Immune Therapy Holdings AB or ITH is a Swedish biotechnology R&D holding company headquartered at the Karolinska Institutet and Karolinska University Hospital in Stockholm.

ITH's research is primarily focused on its Tailored Leukapheresis (TLA) treatment for immune mediated inflammatory diseases (IMIDs).

Wergea (Warga) is a village in the municipality of Leeuwarden in Friesland, Netherlands. It was first mentioned in 944. Wergea developed into a trade and shipping center in the 16th and 17th centuries. In 2021, it had a population of 1,830.

The Romance of Tiger and Rose (Chinese: 传闻中的陈芊芊; pinyin: Chuánwén Zhōng De Chén Qiānqiān) is a 2020 Chinese streaming television series starring Zhao Lusi and Ding Yuxi, and tells the story of a young screenwriter who must survive after being trapped in her own script, with her character meant to die early in the story. The series airs on Tencent Video starting May 18, 2020.

The series became a hit on social media, with its hashtag garnering over 3 billion hits on Weibo. By June 2, the series received over 897 million views on Tencent Video.

The Sumatra Railway was a railway line constructed in Sumatra, Indonesia. The first segments were built between 1924 and 1930, connecting Muarakalaban to Muaro. The project was revived during World War II by the Imperial Japanese Army to connect the east and west coasts of Sumatra, avoiding Allied warships. Construction of this 246-kilometre (153 mi) line from Muaro to Pekanbaru began on May 24, 1944, and was completed on August 15, 1945, the day of Japan's surrender.

The railway was built using rōmusha (conscripted laborers) and approximately 6,600 prisoners of war (POWs), primarily Dutch Indo-Europeans, but also including British, American, Australian, and New Zealand prisoners, spread across 14 camps. By the end of construction, approximately 703 POWs and 20,000 Indonesian romusha had died due to harsh conditions, overwork, malnutrition, and disease.

The Muaro–Pekanbaru railway was never fully utilized and fell into disuse and disrepair. A memorial commemorating the POWs and conscripted laborers who died during the railway's construction was unveiled at the National Memorial Arboretum in Alrewas, England, on VJ Day in 2001. The railway is comparable to other infamous railway projects undertaken by the Japanese during the war, such as the Burma Railway and Kra Isthmus Railway.
John Graham (born between 1954 and 1957) is a Canadian activist and member of the American Indian Movement (AIM). He was convicted of felony murder in the 1975 death of Anna Mae Aquash.
Oliver County is a county in the U.S. state of North Dakota. As of the 2020 census, the population was 1,877. Its county seat and largest city is Center.

Climax is a village in Kalamazoo County in the U.S. state of Michigan. The population was 767 at the 2010 census. The village is located within Climax Township on the northern boundary with Charleston Township, and is roughly 12 miles (19 km) west of Battle Creek and 16 miles (26 km) east of Kalamazoo. Climax shares a school district with the neighboring town of Scotts.

Acacia parramattensis, commonly known as Parramatta wattle or Sydney green wattle, is a tall shrub or tree endemic to central New South Wales. Reaching 15 m (50 ft) in height, it has dark green, divided leaves and yellow flowers that appear from November to February.

First described by Mary Tindale in 1962, it is found in dry sclerophyll forests and woodlands, often alongside eucalyptus trees. While the plant is killed by high intensity fires, it regenerates from seed or basal shoots. The seeds and foliage are food sources for native birds and insects. It is not commonly cultivated, but grows quickly and can provide shade.

Queen's Fort Military Museum is a museum in Bloemfontein, South Africa, which depicts all major wars in the Free State from the 1800s. The building was erected in 1848 and put to use for the first time in 1849. Along the years, it has been used as military headquarters, a hospital, an asylum or mental institution, and a prison.
Wigstan (died 839) was a Mercian nobleman who declined the kingship to pursue a religious life. He was later murdered and became a saint.

The National Democratic Party (NDP) was a far-right political party in the United Kingdom, formed in 1966. It was founded by Dr. David Brown, who had previously used the name in elections. The party expanded through mergers with smaller movements but was gone by the late 1970s.

Margaret JoBeth Williams (born December 6, 1948) is an American actress and television director. Her directorial debut with the 1994 short film On Hope earned her an Academy Award nomination for Best Live Action Short Film. In 2009 she began serving as president of the Screen Actors Guild Foundation; she is President Emeritus of the foundation.

Williams rose to prominence appearing in such films as Stir Crazy (1980), Poltergeist (1982), The Big Chill (1983), The Day After (1983), Teachers (1984), and Poltergeist II: The Other Side (1986). A three-time Emmy Award nominee, she was nominated for Outstanding Lead Actress in a Miniseries or a Movie for her work in the TV movie Adam (1983) and the TV miniseries Baby M (1988). Her third nomination was for her guest role in the sitcom Frasier (1994). She also starred in the TV series The Client (1995–96) and had recurring roles in the TV series Dexter (2007) and Private Practice (2009–11).
Paulus Vallius (Paolo Valla, Paulus Valla, Paulus de la Valle, Paulus de Valle) (1561-1622) was an Italian Jesuit logician.

Wally Bear and the NO! Gang is an educational Nintendo Entertainment System game that was released in 1992 exclusively for a North American audience. It was not licensed by Nintendo. The game teaches children to say no to potentially harmful drugs like tobacco, alcohol and marijuana. Wally Bear and the NO! Gang was designed in cooperation with, and endorsed by, the American Medical Association and the National Clearinghouse for Alcohol and Drug Information.

"Greeny" is the nickname of a 1959 Gibson Les Paul Standard guitar notable for its unique tone and history. It was originally owned by Peter Green of Fleetwood Mac, who used it extensively to record many of the band's early hits, including "Oh Well" and "Black Magic Woman". Green sold the guitar to Gary Moore in 1970, who used it throughout his career, most notably on his signature song "Parisienne Walkways" and with Thin Lizzy.

The guitar's distinctive sound is largely attributed to a flipped alnico magnet in the neck pickup, creating an out-of-phase tone when the pickup selector switch is in the middle position. This anomaly, along with other modifications, contributes to its unique sonic character.

After Moore faced financial troubles, he sold the guitar in 2006. Metallica guitarist Kirk Hammett purchased Greeny in 2014 and has since used it extensively in the studio and during live performances, including a Peter Green tribute concert in 2020. Due to its unique pickup configuration, flipping the neck pickup has been referred to as "the Peter Green mod". In 2022, Monty's Guitars and Seymour Duncan released humbucker pickups modeled after those in Greeny. Gibson has also released several reproductions of the guitar.

Lake Temescal is a reservoir in Oakland, California, created in 1868 by damming Temescal Creek. Originally a drinking water source, it is now a recreational area within the East Bay Regional Park District, offering swimming, fishing, and trails.

NA-36 Hangu-cum-Orakzai is a constituency for the National Assembly of Pakistan. The current representative is Nadeem Khayal of the Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf (PTI), who won the seat in a 2022 by-election after the death of Khayal Zaman Orakzai. General elections were held in 2002, 2008, 2013, and 2018.

Matt Daniels (born September 27, 1989) is an American football coach and former safety. He played college football at Duke University, where he earned All-American honors. Daniels played in the NFL from 2012 to 2015. He is currently the special teams coordinator for the Minnesota Vikings.

Harry Kroger is an American physicist and electrical engineer. He used to be a Bartle professor of electrical engineering at Binghamton University, a part of the State University of New York (SUNY) system. He has been a member of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) since 1964 and became a Life Fellow of the IEEE in 2001. He initially retired to Florida, then moved back to Austin Texas.

Harry Kroger (1935 - September 2022) was an American physicist and electrical engineer. He received a Ph.D. in physics from Cornell University in 1962. Kroger held research and management positions at Sperry Research Center and MCC, and was a professor of electrical engineering at Binghamton University. He held multiple patents in superconductive and semiconducting technologies.

Yankee Dutch crossing is the name of a dance performed in a 4-by-4 square formation of 8 couples (16 dancers). The original version, called simply Dutch Crossing, was choreographed in the English country dance style by Ernst van Brakel of the Netherlands in 1990. In 2004, contra dance caller Joseph Pimentel modified the choreography in the American style, sometimes called Yankee Dutch crossing to distinguish it from the original. Any number of squares can perform the dance simultaneously. It is not normally found at monthly, bi-monthly or weekly dances. The most likely venues are multi-day dance weekends or weeks, where it is taught in sessions of one hour or longer.

The Mourne Mountains (/mɔːrn/ MORN; Lang-ga), also called the Mournes or Mountains of Mourne, are a granite mountain range in County Down in the south-east of Northern Ireland. They include the highest mountains in Northern Ireland, the highest of which is Slieve Donard at 850 m (2,790 ft). The Mournes are designated an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty and it has been proposed to make the area Northern Ireland's first national park. The area is partly owned by the National Trust and sees many visitors every year. The Mourne Wall crosses fifteen of the summits and was built to enclose the catchment basin of the Silent Valley and Ben Crom reservoirs.

Rod Diridon Sr. (born February 8, 1939) is an American politician known for his leadership in mass transportation. He served as chair of the California High-Speed Rail Authority from 2001-2003. He currently chairs the advisory board of the United States High Speed Rail Association, and he chairs the National Heritage Area Project for Santa Clara County, California. The New York Times calls Diridon "a tireless advocate of public transport for the Bay Area."

Diridon has also been active in Silicon Valley in leadership on environmental issues, the arts and culture, higher education, community organizations, and philanthropy.
USS *R-14* (SS-91) was an R-class submarine of the United States Navy, commissioned in 1919 and decommissioned in 1945.

Patricia Murphy Derian (August 12, 1929 – May 20, 2016) was an American civil rights activist and public official. She served as the Assistant Secretary of State for Human Rights and Humanitarian Affairs from 1977 to 1981, during the Carter administration. In this role, she worked to improve policy coordination on human rights, refugees, and prisoners of war.

Major General Hurdis Secundus Lalande Ravenshaw CMG (June 1869 – c. 6 June 1920) was a senior British Army officer in the First World War who served at the Royal Military College, Sandhurst and saw action on the North-West Frontier of India, in South Africa in the Second Boer War and in France and Greece in the First World War. In 1916 a German U-boat captured him, and he was a prisoner of war for the next two years. He died in 1920 in unusual circumstances after becoming lost in the South African bush near Port Elizabeth and succumbing to the elements.
John Leslie Sissons (born 30 September 1945) is an English former footballer who played as a forward for West Ham United and other clubs.

The Amos Baldwin House is a historic house at 92 Goshen Street East in Norfolk, Connecticut, United States. Built about 1765, it is an important surviving example of colonial architecture in the community, and is one of its oldest buildings with a gambrel roof. It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2016.

The 2022 Argentine Torneo Federal A, was the tenth season of the Torneo Federal A, the regionalised third tier of the Argentine football league system. The tournament is reserved for teams indirectly affiliated to the Asociación del Fútbol Argentino (AFA), while teams affiliated to AFA have to play the Primera B Metropolitana, which is the other third tier competition. The competition was contested by 29 of the 31 teams that took part in the 2021 season and four teams promoted from Torneo Regional Federal Amateur. Two teams will be promoted to Primera Nacional and four teams were relegated to Torneo Regional Federal Amateur. The season began on 27 march and ended in 13 November 2022.

Jefferson Andrés Lerma Solís (born October 25, 1994) is a Colombian professional footballer who plays as a defensive midfielder for Premier League club AFC Bournemouth and the Colombia national team.

Lerma began his senior career with Atlético Huila in 2013, before a loan to Levante in 2015 became permanent in 2016. In 2018, he joined AFC Bournemouth for a club record fee.

Internationally, Lerma has represented Colombia at the Olympic level in 2016 and made his senior debut for Colombia in 2017. He was part of the Colombia squad for the 2018 FIFA World Cup and the 2019 Copa América, scoring his first international goal in 2020.
